title: Aceitunas Rellenas (Stuffed Olives)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- large green olives
- toasted bread, grated
- fresh cheese
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, ground
- white broth with white wine
- butter
- white onion, chopped
- parsley
- pickled gherkins
- cumin
- tomato

## Directions

1. Use the largest green olives. Cut the lower extremity and through there, with the tip of a sharp knife, extract the pit without damaging the shape of the fruit.
2. Fill with a sauce made of grated toasted bread, fresh cheese and hard-boiled egg yolks, broken down and kneaded with oil and vinegar, seasoned with salt, pepper and ground parsley.
3. Cover the filling with the cut piece, securing it with very thin small stakes made from thin cane strips.
4. Cook in good broth saturated with white wine; once cooked in one boil, remove from the broth and give them another boil in a sauce made of butter, white onion, parsley and pickled gherkins: all very finely chopped and seasoned with pepper, salt, a little cumin, oil, vinegar and one tablespoon of tomato.

title: Acelgas a la Noemi (Chard in the Style of Noemi)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- chard
- butter
- one spoonful of flour
- a pinch of sugar
- pepper
- salt
- one cup of good milk
- lemon juice
- bread fried in butter

## Directions

1. After cooking, grind the chard, and once well ground, fry it in butter with a spoonful of flour, a pinch of sugar, pepper and salt.
2. Once fried, pour over it a cup of good milk and let it cook until it reduces to a thick sauce.
3. Squeeze a little lemon juice into it and serve with slices of bread fried in butter on top as garnish.

title: Adobo a la Hojaldra (Pork Adobo in Puff Pastry)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of pork adobo: ribs or sirloin
- Pork fat for frying
- Puff pastry sheets

## Directions

1. Cut regular pieces of pork adobo, either ribs or sirloin.
2. Flatten them one by one by pounding them with a heavy object: the pestle of a mortar or pounding tool.
3. Fry them in pork fat in an enameled porcelain pan or glazed earthenware pan.
4. Once cooked, arrange them in a serving dish between two layers of puff pastry.
5. Place in an oven that should be at strong heat due to the short cooking time required.

title: Adobo de Chancho (Marinated Pork)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of good vinegar
- four clove heads of garlic, ground
- pepper
- cumin, ground
- one onion head, finely minced
- oregano powder
- salt
- two bay leaves
- pork loin or ribs

## Directions

1. In a porcelain or terracotta vessel, pour one liter of good vinegar
2. Add four clove heads of garlic ground, pepper, cumin also ground, one finely minced onion head, oregano powder, salt, and two bay leaves
3. Grind well, stirring with a wooden spoon
4. Submerge the pieces of meat in this mixture, turning them in it
5. Cover the vessel and keep the meat in this adobo for three hours
6. Hang it to air-dry in a cool, shaded place with a current of air
7. Roast this adobo in the oven; it is a delicious dish

title: Adobo de Chancho (Pork Adobo)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of good vinegar
- four heads of garlic, ground
- pepper
- cumin, ground
- one head of onion, finely chopped
- oregano powder
- salt
- two bay leaves
- pork loin or ribs
- butter

## Directions

1. In a porcelain or terra-cotta vessel, pour one liter of good vinegar, four heads of ground garlic, pepper, ground cumin, one finely chopped head of onion, oregano powder, salt, and two bay leaves
2. Mix well, stirring with a wooden spoon, and submerge the meat pieces in this mixture, turning them in it
3. Cover the vessel and keep the meat in this adobo for three hours, then hang it to air dry in a cool, shady place with air circulation
4. Place the adobo in an oven-safe dish with butter at the bottom and roast in the oven

title: Albóndigas de Cordero (Lamb Meatballs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from lamb leg
- Bacon
- Parsley
- One clove of garlic
- Bread soaked in milk and ground
- Black pepper
- A little cumin
- Salt to taste
- Eggs
- Flour
- Boiling broth

## Directions

1. Chop the meat from the lamb leg together with bacon, parsley, and one clove of garlic
2. Add bread soaked in milk and ground, pepper, a little cumin, salt to taste, and eggs
3. Mix and knead to make a mass
4. Shape into balls the size of hen eggs
5. Coat them in flour so they do not fall apart
6. Cook them in boiling broth for about fifteen minutes at a vigorous boil
7. Remove from heat when done

title: Albóndigas de Cordero (Lamb Meatballs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb, minced
- bacon
- parsley
- one clove of garlic
- bread soaked in milk and ground
- pepper
- a little cumin
- salt to taste
- eggs
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- cream
- onions
- vinegar
- oil
- hard-boiled eggs
- pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. Mince the meat from the leg with bacon, parsley, and a clove of garlic
2. Add bread soaked in milk and ground, pepper, a little cumin, salt to taste, and eggs
3. Mix and work into a dough, forming it into the shape of hen eggs
4. Cook in boiling broth, rolled in flour so they do not fall apart
5. After a quarter hour of vigorous boiling, remove from heat and place on the serving dish
6. Coat with a sauce made of breadcrumbs dissolved in cream, cooked and sliced onions, pepper, vinegar, and oil
7. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut in half and pickled gherkins

title: Alcachofas a la Sarita (Artichokes in the Style of Sarita)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- cold cooked meat
- chorizo
- raisins
- hard-boiled eggs
- celery
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- oil

## Directions

1. After cooking the artichokes, remove the leaves of the first row and cut off the top heart with a knife.
2. Round them well and remove the central part.
3. Prepare a filling of cold cooked meat, crumbled chorizo, raisins, chopped hard-boiled eggs, celery, parsley, and breadcrumbs, all finely chopped.
4. Fill the artichokes and arrange them in a pan that resists heat.
5. Pour half a spoonful of oil over each one, cover the filling with half a hard-boiled egg, and cook them covered in steam.

title: Ambrosia (Ambrosia)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of good milk
- one pound of sugar
- twelve egg yolks
- six egg whites, well beaten
- zest of the skin of two lemons
- one small glass of Port wine (or curacao)

## Directions

1. Mix one liter of good milk with one pound of sugar and place on the heat
2. When it begins to boil, add twelve egg yolks and six well-beaten egg whites, and the zest of the skin of two lemons
3. Stir this mixture over a lively fire until a green syrup appears on the surface
4. Pour into a pudding mold that has been previously buttered
5. Place in the oven with moderate heat and rapid cooking
6. At the moment the mass begins to pull away from the sides of the pudding mold, sprinkle a small glass of the best wine, using Port wine as the measure (curacao is more recommended for this pudding)
7. Cool and serve

title: Ante (Fruit Compote)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- dried prunes
- whole peeled almonds
- dried peach halves
- preserved cherries
- Málaga raisins
- whole cloves
- cinnamon sticks
- sugar
- water
- 1 ripe pineapple
- ripe peaches
- golden oranges
- 3 tablespoons of Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Cook dried prunes, whole peeled almonds, dried peach halves, preserved cherries, Málaga raisins, whole cloves, and cinnamon sticks with sugar and sufficient water as for compote
2. Remove from fire and let cool
3. Choose a ripe pineapple, peel it, and cut into thin slices
4. Choose the best peaches and oranges of deep golden color and very fresh, peel and cut into small slices
5. Prepare a deep glass or porcelain vessel and arrange by layers: first one of fruit, over it a dusting of sugar and cinnamon, sprinkling with 3 tablespoons of the best Sherry wine, and another layer of compote, and another of fruit, with equal dusting of sugar and cinnamon, and sprinkled with wine
6. The last layer must be of fruit, then fill the container with wine until the fruit is covered
7. Leave from one day to the next, soaked in liquor, fruits and compote, and serve in glasses
8. Used for lunch

title: Anticuchos a la Última (Modern-Style Lamb Kidney Skewers)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- garlic
- mirasol chili pepper (or red chili pepper)
- fresh bacon
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash the kidneys in cold water.
2. Prick them with a thick needle and soak them for half an hour in water seasoned with salt.
3. Place them in a marinade of oil and vinegar, pepper, minced garlic, and powdered mirasol chili pepper (or red chili if mirasol is not available), for two hours.
4. After marinating, split the kidneys in half.
5. Mix them with very thin slices of fresh bacon.
6. Thread them on a thin wooden skewer.
7. Place over a strong fire, turning constantly.
8. Brush continuously with butter mixed with a spoonful of marinade using a feather.
9. Cook quickly and serve very hot.

title: Anticuchos de Tortolitas (Roasted Doves on Skewers)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Beef stock
- Butter
- Grated bread
- Buttered paper
- Yellow chili sauce or English mustard

## Directions

1. Cook the doves in good beef stock until well done.
2. Remove from the stock and let cool.
3. Dry them, coat with butter, and roll in grated bread.
4. Wrap in buttered paper.
5. Place in the oven until golden.
6. Serve with yellow chili sauce or English mustard.

title: Arroz (Rice)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of lard
- a small piece of chopped onion
- one clove of garlic
- salt
- one pound of rice, washed in several waters
- cold water

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped onion, garlic clove, and salt in four ounces of lard until the sauce is well cooked
2. Add one pound of rice that has been washed in several waters to remove the flour it generally contains, and mix it with the lard
3. Immediately, before the rice cooks, add cold water until the rice is covered
4. Cover very well and cook over low heat for half an hour
5. The rice will then be grainy and very well cooked

title: Arroz a la High Life (High Life Rice)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of good white rice, well washed three times
- two cups of milk
- a cinnamon stick
- a piece of lemon
- milk for cooking
- one pound of sugar
- one small cup of walnut paste
- walnuts, peeled
- milk for the walnut paste
- cinnamon and powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of good white rice, well washed three times, for six hours. Wash it once more and add to a pan with two cups of milk, a cinnamon stick, and a piece of lemon. Allow to cook, having ready a pitcher of milk to add as the rice absorbs the milk during cooking and begins to swell.
2. When almost cooked, add one pound of sugar without stopping stirring, using a piece of sugarcane cut for this purpose and stirring with only one hand; if another person takes over the stirring, the milk in the rice will curdle. Bring to a high point, then remove from heat and mix in one small cup of walnut paste that has been prepared by grinding the peeled walnuts very well and mixing with milk while grinding to prevent it from turning black. After grinding, add a bit more milk and strain through a sieve. Add to the rice while still off the heat, stir very well, and serve on a round platter with a dusting of cinnamon and powdered sugar.

title: Arroz Graneado (Fluffy Rice)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork fat, 4 ounces
- Onion, a small piece, chopped
- Garlic clove, 1
- Salt
- Rice, 1 pound, washed
- Water, cold

## Directions

1. Fry a small piece of chopped onion, one garlic clove, and salt in four ounces of pork fat.
2. When this sauce is well cooked, add one pound of rice which should be washed several times until stripped of the flour it generally has.
3. Mix it with the fat and immediately, before the rice cooks, pour cold water over it until it is covered.
4. Cover very well and cook over low heat for half an hour; the rice will then be fluffy and well cooked.

title: Asado de Costillas (Roasted Ribs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cross-cut piece of cow ribs, quadrilateral in shape
- cold water
- broth conditioned with salt
- garlic and onion, ground
- tomato sauce
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- green chiles, sliced

## Directions

1. Cut across the rib cage of a cow a quadrilateral piece of the size needed
2. Wash it with cold water and after drying it, turn it in a plate of broth seasoned with plenty of salt and a pinch of ground garlic and onion
3. Let it drain and extend it on the grill on the bone side, over a very lively fire
4. When the bones are toasted, prepare another bed of equally lively coals
5. Transfer the grill to the new bed of coals, quickly turning the piece to the meat side, which is already almost cooked through the ribs
6. With five more minutes of fire it is ready to serve on a liquid tomato sauce, seasoned with oil, vinegar, pepper, salt and sliced green chiles

title: Asado de Ternera (Roasted Veal)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of veal, rib, sirloin or leg
- vinegar
- olive oil
- two heads of onion
- one head of garlic, ground
- pepper
- salt
- oregano
- cumin, pulverized

## Directions

1. Place the piece of veal, rib, sirloin or leg in a wooden, earthen or porcelain vessel for six hours
2. Marinate it in a mixture of vinegar, olive oil, two heads of onion and one of garlic ground, pepper, salt, oregano and pulverized cumin
3. Dry it and place it on the grill or in a well-heated oven, as cooking should be rapid
4. Serve with salad

title: Bacalao a la Crema (Cod in Cream Sauce)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of cod, well washed and desalted
- Butter, seasoned with pepper
- One cup of cream, or in its absence, good milk
- Bread crumbs
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of cod in water, well washed and desalted, and remove the skin and spines.
2. Make a white sauce of butter seasoned with pepper; mix in one cup of cream, and in its absence, good milk.
3. Add the cod to this sauce and let it boil so that it thickens.
4. Then even out the surface and cover it with a layer of bread crumbs.
5. Over this bread crumbs pour melted butter.
6. Another layer of bread crumbs with melted butter on top, allow it to brown in the kitchen oven or in a portable stove.
7. Serve very hot, with small slices of bread fried in butter around it.

title: Blanquete a la Lionesa (Lionaise Blanquette of Veal)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- onions
- carrot
- salt
- whole pepper
- water
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- cebolleta (scallion)
- nutmeg
- ground pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut regular square pieces from the veal breast.
2. Place them in a pan with two onions, one carrot, salt, and whole pepper.
3. Cover the meat with water and bring to boil, removing the foam.
4. After two boils needed to skim, place the pan to simmer slowly in half the broth in which it has cooked.
5. Use the other half of the broth for the sauce by passing it through a sieve.
6. In a dish put four ounces of butter, one spoonful of flour, a small amount of chopped parsley, a scallion cut into pieces, salt, pepper, a small grating of nutmeg, and the carrot that has cooked with the meat, cut into rounds.
7. Place over heat and cook until the onion and parsley soften.
8. Add some of the broth passed through the sieve, which should be kept warm near the fire.
9. Add then one spoonful of ground pepper, and after a boil of ten minutes, add a glass of good milk.
10. Stir well, add the pieces of veal and let cook for a quarter of an hour, time necessary for it to be ready to serve.

title: Bocadillos a la Princesa (Princess Pastries)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- good beef cuts, proportioned to each diner's portion
- garlic, well ground
- cumin
- pepper
- a slice of bacon
- chopped onion
- tomato
- well ground peanut
- almonds
- walnuts
- salt
- butter
- onions cut into slices
- tomatoes, seeded and cut into pieces
- half bottle of good Priorat wine
- half bottle of vinegar
- a very small amount of salt
- one ounce of grated chocolate
- parsley and mint for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut proportioned pieces from good beef
2. Pound them in a mortar with four strikes, just enough to extend the meat without breaking it apart
3. Place them on a cloth over a board
4. Coat them with ground garlic and cumin
5. Sprinkle with pepper
6. Place at the center a small slice of bacon, chopped onion, tomato, and very well ground peanut, almonds, walnuts and salt
7. Roll each piece and wrap with white thread
8. Arrange in an enameled porcelain pot over a layer of butter, sliced onions, seeded and cut tomatoes
9. Add half a bottle of good Priorat wine, half bottle of vinegar, very little salt, and one ounce of grated chocolate
10. Cover the pot and cook over low heat
11. Uncover frequently to check if the liquid evaporates too much, and add hot water bit by bit
12. After two hours of cooking, let it dry a bit and serve with garnish of parsley and mint leaves

title: Bocadillos a la Sucrense (Potato Fritters)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen chuños (frozen potatoes)
- three onion heads
- one tomato
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- cumin
- aji powder
- butter
- eight egg yolks
- pork fat

## Directions

1. Soak two dozen chuños for two days.
2. Wash them thoroughly in several waters and cook them.
3. Once well cooked, squeeze them and grind them.
4. Cook a sauce in butter made with three onion heads, a seeded tomato, and a handful of parsley.
5. Grind everything finely and season with salt, pepper, cumin, and a bit of aji powder if desired.
6. Mix the potato paste and sauce vigorously so they incorporate well.
7. Add a beaten mixture of eight egg yolks.
8. Fry by spoonfuls in pork fat, flattening the mass with the back of the spoon so it takes the shape of a fritter.
9. Serve with a garnish of candied eggs.

title: Bocadillos de Crema (Cream Sandwiches)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen puff pastry pieces (hojaldra)
- Cream, beaten with sugar and cinnamon
- Very ripe strawberries

## Directions

1. Bake two dozen puff pastry pieces on a baking sheet in the oven until lightly golden.
2. When done, remove from the oven while still hot.
3. Place one dozen on the serving dish.
4. Spread one full spoonful of cream (beaten with sugar and cinnamon) on each piece.
5. Cover with the other dozen puff pastry pieces.
6. Cover the top with a layer of very ripe strawberries covering the entire dish.
7. Serve.

title: Borrachitos a la Mussett (Mussett's Little Drunken Cakes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of sponge cake
- Burgundy wine
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak slices of sponge cake in Burgundy wine and arrange in layers in a heat-resistant porcelain dish. Each layer should be lightly dusted with sugar and cinnamon. Once filled, place the dish in the oven at moderate heat for half an hour.
2. This dessert is very pleasant served hot or cold, and more than justifies its name.

title: Budín (Savory Egg and Pasta Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork fat
- Breadcrumbs
- Pasta
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Coat the pudding mold entirely with pork fat and sprinkle breadcrumbs on top
2. Place a layer of pasta in the bottom
3. Break an egg in the center over the pasta and sprinkle generously with breadcrumbs
4. Add another layer of pasta and another egg, continuing to alternate layers until the mold is full
5. Close the mold and place in a water bath in a pot with water filled halfway, bringing to a boil over good heat
6. Cover and place hot ashes and coals on top of the lid
7. After one hour of cooking, the pudding is ready
8. Turn it out onto the serving dish and pour the desired sauce over it

title: Budín a la Condesa (Countess Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- forty egg yolks
- two pounds of ground sugar
- half a pound of ground almonds
- half a pound of sugar
- two sponge cakes of twenty centimeters
- half a pound of cream

## Directions

1. To well-beaten egg yolks, mix two pounds of ground sugar, half a pound of ground almonds with half a pound of sugar, two sponge cakes of twenty centimeters, equally ground, and half a pound of cream.
2. Once all incorporated, stir and beat until it forms a smooth paste.
3. Arrange it in a mold buttered with butter and place it in the oven.

title: Budín a la Doveière (Doveière Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- raw bacon
- white onion
- four eggs
- butter
- parsley
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Chop very finely a piece of parboiled veal, and an equal piece of raw bacon, ground. Chop and fry white onion. Beat separately whites and yolks of four eggs.
2. When the whites are stiff, mix everything; add a spoonful of butter, chopped parsley and thyme, season with salt and pepper, and stir and beat well, until forming a paste.
3. Grease the entire mold with lard, sprinkle breadcrumbs on top, and place a layer of paste on the bottom. Over it, in the center, break an egg, and on top a strong sprinkling of breadcrumbs, and another layer of paste and another egg, and so on until filling the mold, which is closed and placed in a water bath, in a pan that with water up to the middle will be boiling on good heat. Cover it, and place hot ash and embers on the lid.
4. After one hour of cooking, the pudding is ready. Turn it out onto the platter to take it to the table, after pouring over it the sauce you wish.

title: Budín a la Jardinera (Vegetable Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half dozen spinach leaves, cleaned
- Water
- Bread crumbs soaked in milk
- Six egg yolks
- Butter
- Four egg whites
- Salt and pepper
- Toasted bread or bread fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cook a dozen and a half clean spinach leaves in unsalted water
2. Once well cooked, mash with a spoon until it resembles a paste
3. Mix in bread crumbs soaked and mashed in milk
4. Add six egg yolks beaten with a piece of butter and four whites beaten to stiff peaks
5. Combine well, season with salt and pepper
6. Butter the pudding mold and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
7. Fill with the mixture and place in the oven
8. Serve with toasted bread on the grill or fried in butter

title: Budín a la Mita (Mita Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of milk
- five ounces of sugar
- a small piece of vanilla
- zest of the skin of one lemon
- two small sticks of cinnamon
- seven ounces of starch dissolved in milk
- seven egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put a liter of milk to boil with five ounces of sugar, a small piece of vanilla, the zest of the skin of one lemon, and two small sticks of cinnamon.
2. When it has come to a boil, remove it from the fire, pass it through a sieve, and return the milk to the fire, adding, little by little, seven ounces of starch dissolved in milk.
3. Stir, bringing it to a boil for ten minutes.
4. Let it cool, and add seven well-beaten egg yolks.
5. Place it on the fire for a moment, without stopping beating, and empty it into the pudding mold to cool.

title: Budín a la Monona (Monona-Style Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Six eggs, whites and yolks
- Butter
- Onion
- Garlic
- Tomato
- Rice, cooked in unsalted water
- Gruyère cheese, grated
- Two dozen fresh almonds, ground
- Milk
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Beat together the whites and yolks of six eggs
2. Heat butter in a pan that has been previously fried with onion, garlic, and tomato, then strained through a sieve to remove all these ingredients
3. Pour the beaten eggs into the hot butter and stir well while cooking, seasoning with salt and pepper
4. Cook the rice in unsalted water and drain it in a napkin to extract all water
5. Mix the cooked rice with grated Gruyère cheese to season, and add two dozen fresh almonds ground and moistened while grinding with a few drops of milk
6. Butter the pudding mold
7. Spread a layer of the rice and almond mixture on the bottom
8. Add a layer of the scrambled egg on top
9. Alternate layers, ensuring the last layer on top is rice
10. Brush lightly with a small amount of butter and place in the oven
11. When the pudding takes on a deep golden color, it is properly cooked and can be served

title: Budín a la Parnell (Parnell Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- three cups of ground and sifted sugar
- four cups of wheat flour
- one cup of butter
- four eggs
- one tablespoon of soda
- one tablespoon of cream of tartar
- one small glass of aniseed liqueur
- one teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
- zest of the skin of one lemon
- half a pound of currants
- a little dried lemon preserve, crumbled

## Directions

1. Beat together: one cup of milk, three cups of ground and sifted sugar, four cups of wheat flour, one cup of butter, four eggs, one tablespoon of soda, one tablespoon of cream of tartar, one small glass of aniseed liqueur, one teaspoon of powdered cinnamon, the zest of the skin of one lemon, half a pound of currants, and a little dried lemon preserve, crumbled.
2. Once well beaten and incorporated, place it in a mold buttered with butter and put it in the oven, which should be very hot.

title: Budín a la Platense (Platense-Style Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One pejerrey fish
- Salt
- One onion head
- Peppercorns
- Bread crumbs dissolved in milk
- Four eggs, whites and yolks
- Butter
- Pepper
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cook a pejerrey with salt, an onion head, and some peppercorns
2. Once cooked, carefully remove the bones and spines, shredding the fish well with your hands to find any remaining spines
3. Mix with bread crumbs dissolved in milk and the whites and yolks of four eggs beaten separately: the whites whipped to stiff peaks, the yolks beaten with one ounce of unmelted butter
4. Stir and combine, seasoning with pepper and salt
5. Butter the pudding mold, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, fill with the prepared mixture, and place in the oven
6. Serve with hard-boiled eggs cut in half and thin slices of bread toasted on the grill

title: Budín a la Reina (Queen Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one beer bread
- butter
- quince jelly
- five egg yolks
- five egg whites
- five ounces of sugar
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut a beer bread into slices. Spread half of these with butter, and the other half with quince jelly.
2. Butter the pudding mold and arrange the slices in layers: one with butter over another with jelly.
3. Beat five egg yolks and the whites with five ounces of sugar and enough milk to cover the slices and fill the mold.
4. Pour over the slices, let soak for two hours, and place in the oven.

title: Budín a la Salesa (Salesa-Style Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Gruyère cheese, grated
- Four egg yolks
- One dozen almonds
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Toast the rice, then wash it well and cook in unsalted water
2. Once well cooked, drain in a napkin to extract the water
3. Mix with grated Gruyère cheese, four egg yolks, one dozen finely ground almonds, pepper, and very little salt because of the cheese
4. Stir and mix everything very well
5. Place in the oven at moderate heat

title: Budín de Carne (Meat Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the loin or ham
- Six eggs
- Six peeled and skinned walnuts
- A piece of toasted bread, grated
- One ounce of butter
- Pepper
- Almonds
- Málaga raisins
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Tomato sauce, liquid

## Directions

1. Parboil a piece of beef from the loin or ham
2. Chop it finely, then grind it in a mortar; shred it, remove nerves and skin, and grind and shred again
3. Season with pepper, one tablespoon of vinegar and salt, and let it marinate for one hour
4. Beat six eggs
5. Grind six peeled and skinned walnuts
6. Grate a piece of toasted bread
7. Mix everything together, stirring very well, and add one ounce of butter, pepper, almonds, and Málaga raisins
8. Butter the pudding mold on the bottom and sides and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
9. Fill with the prepared mixture and place in an oven at moderate heat
10. Serve with liquid tomato sauce

title: Budín de Limón (Lemon Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters cup of rich milk
- 2 ounces of butter
- 4 ounces of sugar
- half teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
- 10 ounces of bread crumbs
- zest of the peel of 1 lemon
- 4 eggs
- 1 large tablespoon of good cognac
- butter for the mold

## Directions

1. Boil three-quarters cup of milk
2. When it has boiled, add 2 ounces of butter and 4 ounces of sugar with half a teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
3. Mix with 10 ounces of bread crumbs and the zest of lemon peel
4. Beat 4 eggs and add them along with 1 large tablespoon of good cognac
5. Butter the mold, place the pudding in it, and put in the oven at moderate heat to cook for one hour

title: Budín de Limón (Lemon Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a liter of rich milk
- two ounces of butter
- four ounces of sugar
- half a teaspoon of cinnamon powder
- ten ounces of bread crumbs
- zest of the skin of one lemon
- four eggs
- one large tablespoon of good cognac
- raisins from Málaga
- cinnamon candies

## Directions

1. Boil three-quarters of a liter of rich milk. When it has boiled, add two ounces of butter and four ounces of sugar, with half a teaspoon of cinnamon powder.
2. Mix with ten ounces of bread crumbs and the zest of the skin of one lemon (that is, the zest of the skin, not the pith).
3. Beat four eggs and add them, together with one large tablespoon of good cognac.
4. Butter a mold, arrange the pudding in it, and place it in the oven, where it should cook for one hour.
5. Serve studding it with raisins from Málaga and cinnamon candies.

title: Budín de Novia (Bride Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of bread yeast from a bakery
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- ten tablespoons of powdered sugar
- six tablespoons of butter
- one pound of flour
- a sufficient quantity of cream
- ground almonds
- syrup mixed with Jamaica rum

## Directions

1. Take a piece of bread yeast from a bakery and dissolve it in two tablespoons of wheat flour; stir very well and, covering the mixture with a cloth, let it ferment for two hours.
2. Then, knead this yeast with ten tablespoons of powdered sugar, six of butter, one pound of flour, and a sufficient quantity of cream to make of this mixture a soft paste.
3. Take a mold of a beautiful shape, cover the bottom with a layer of ground almonds, fill it with the paste, and let it rest for four hours.
4. Bake in a very hot oven.
5. Upon removing it from the mold, sprinkle it with syrup mixed with Jamaica rum.

title: Buñuelos a la Celestina (Celestina-Style Fritters)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound flour (flor de harina)
- Salt
- Five ounces butter
- Twenty egg yolks and eight egg whites, beaten
- Butter for frying
- Red wine syrup
- Cinnamon for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of flour with a pinch of salt and five ounces of butter, stirring constantly during cooking.
2. When it begins to sound when stirred, remove from heat and let cool.
3. When lukewarm, mix twenty beaten egg yolks and eight beaten egg whites into the cooked mixture.
4. Drop the batter by spoonfuls into boiling butter, one spoonful per fritter.
5. As each fritter cooks and browns, remove it from the butter and group them together on a dish under a folded cloth to keep warm.
6. Serve drizzled with red wine syrup and sprinkled with cinnamon.

title: Buñuelos a la Porteña (Buenos Aires-Style Fritters)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Good apricots
- Grape brandy
- Pulverized sugar
- Lemon zest
- Two handfuls flour
- One glass milk
- Butter
- One small glass brandy
- One and a half egg whites, beaten and made into foam
- Salt
- Butter for frying
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Select good apricots, cut them in half, and remove the pits.
2. Dissolve pulverized sugar in quality grape brandy and soak the apricot halves in this mixture along with lemon zest cut into pieces for two hours.
3. Remove the apricot pieces from the brandy and dry them.
4. Make the fritter batter: dissolve two handfuls of flour in a glass of milk, add a piece of butter, stir and beat well, then add a small glass of brandy little by little.
5. When ready to fry, add one and a half egg whites (beaten and made into foam) to the batter along with a pinch of salt.
6. Wrap each apricot piece in the batter, place it on a spoon, and fry in boiling butter.
7. If convenient, place the fritters briefly in the oven so the sprinkled sugar caramelizes, rather than serving them directly from the pan with sugar sprinkled on top.

title: Calabacita Empanada (Stuffed Squash Pastry)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- tomatoes
- onions
- garlic
- green chili
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- saffron
- dried cilantro
- strong vinegar
- raisins
- toasted almonds
- black olives
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Peel the squash and chop it with tomatoes, onions, garlic and green chili, and coat in sufficient butter and salt.
2. Once cooked in this sauce, add a little pepper, saffron and dried cilantro, ground to powder, all dissolved in a little strong vinegar, with raisins, toasted almonds and black olives.
3. Place this filling between two squares of puff pastry, which are roasted in the oven, or fried in butter.

title: Calabacita Empanada (Stuffed Zucchini)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- calabacitas (zucchini)
- tomates (tomatoes)
- cebollas (onions)
- ajos (garlic)
- ají verde (green chili)
- mantequilla (butter)
- sal (salt)
- pimienta (pepper)
- azafrán (saffron)
- culantro seco (dried cilantro)
- vinagre fuerte (strong vinegar)
- pasas de uva (raisins)
- almendras tostadas (toasted almonds)
- aceitunas negras (black olives)

## Directions

1. Peel the zucchini and chop them with tomatoes, onions, garlic, and green chili
2. Coat in sufficient butter and salt
3. Once cooked in that sauce, add a little pepper, saffron, and dried powdered cilantro, all dissolved in a little strong vinegar, with raisins, toasted almonds, and black olives

title: Calabaza al Horno (Roasted Squash)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- salt
- three eggs
- grated cheese
- sauce cooked in pork fat fried with pepper

## Directions

1. Peel the squash and cook it in salted water.
2. Once cooked, dry it in a cloth; mash it with a spoon, crushing and stirring it alternately.
3. Mix in three beaten eggs, grated cheese, and sauce cooked in pork fat fried with pepper.
4. Arrange in a baking dish and place in the oven.

title: Camarones a la Imprevista (Shrimp Prepared Unexpectedly)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp, alive
- Salt
- Bundle of fine herbs
- Butter
- Two onion heads, finely chopped
- Parsley
- One garlic clove, well crushed
- Tomato juice, strained and seasoned with salt and pepper
- Shrimp broth
- Two egg yolks
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Half a teaspoon of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the shrimp in water seasoned with salt and a bundle of fine herbs.
2. Remove the shrimp from the pan and separate the heads from the tails.
3. Peel the tails very well, wash them, and arrange them in a serving dish.
4. Grind the heads in a mortar or pestle and dissolve the resulting paste in a little of the cooking broth, then strain through a sieve.
5. In a pan, fry butter and cook the finely chopped onion heads, parsley, well-crushed garlic clove, and the tomato juice seasoned with salt and pepper.
6. When the onion has taken on color, add the head broth, two egg yolks, and a regular amount of grated Parmesan cheese, ensuring the sauce becomes thick.
7. Add half a teaspoon of powdered sugar to mitigate the saltiness of the cheese.
8. Pour the boiling sauce over the dish containing the shrimp tails and serve hot.

title: Camarones a la Panameña (Panamanian Shrimp)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- three dozen shrimp
- salt
- aromatic herbs, a bunch
- white onion, finely chopped
- tomato, finely chopped
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Fish three dozen shrimp with a small net
2. Cook the shrimp in salted water seasoned with a bunch of aromatic herbs
3. Once cooked, peel the shrimp
4. Take the tails and place them in a serving dish
5. Season with salt, pepper, finely chopped white onion and tomato, bread crumbs, and oil and vinegar
6. Mix and stir everything together carefully and cover until the moment of serving

title: Carapulca a la Valle de Chacaltana (Carapulca from Valle de Chacaltana)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of dried potato, lightly toasted and ground
- Half a pound of well-fatty pork
- Half a pound of pork fat
- Two large onions
- Two tomatoes
- One clove of garlic
- Ten ground chili peppers, or one teaspoon of paprika if no chili is available
- Salt to taste
- Half a pound of roasted peanuts, finely ground
- Pork broth

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of dried potato, lightly toasted and ground, in water
2. Separately cook half a pound of well-fatty pork
3. Heat half a pound of pork fat until boiling and add two finely chopped large onions, two finely cut tomatoes, one clove of garlic, and ten ground chili peppers (or one teaspoon of paprika and salt to taste)
4. Cook the sauce well
5. Add the cooked dried potato and the pork cut into pieces
6. Boil everything together for a while
7. Before removing from heat, add half a pound of roasted peanuts, finely ground and dissolved in a small cup of pork broth
8. Optional: add poultry meat such as turkey, chicken or squabs, and grated butter rosquillas

title: Carne con Cuero (Meat with Hide)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of meat with hide
- linen sack
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the piece of meat in two waters and dry it carefully with a napkin, both meat and hide
2. Place it well extended in a linen sack proportioned to the size of the piece, so that it receives direct contact with the earth from all sides
3. Fold the mouth of the sack and sew it with fine stitches
4. Dig a hole in the garden half a meter deep, with width and length proportioned to the size of the sack
5. Place the sack in the hole with the meat facing down and the hide facing up
6. Fill the hole again and press the surface, leaving it for three hours
7. Extract the meat with the same care with which it was buried
8. Season lightly with salt
9. Take it to the fire where it will be roasted by experts until cooked through, resulting in exquisite meat with hide, tender as if it had been cooked for many hours

title: Causa (Causa)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes as needed
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Ground chili pepper to taste
- Fresh, tender lettuce leaves
- Onions
- Black pepper
- Oregano powder
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Fried fish pieces
- Cooked shrimp
- Fresh cheese slices
- Sweet potatoes or yams
- Olives
- Radishes
- Lettuce hearts

## Directions

1. Cook the necessary potatoes and peel them, removing their skin
2. Grind them as if making purée
3. Mix the potato mass with plenty of olive oil, vinegar, salt, and ground chili pepper to taste
4. Line the serving dish with a layer of fresh, tender, well-washed lettuce leaves
5. Spread the potato mixture on these leaves in the form of a cake
6. Prepare a sauce by cutting onions into very thin rounds and pickling them for three hours in vinegar, olive oil, black pepper, powdered oregano, and chili if desired
7. Place these pickled onions on top of the causa
8. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut into slices, pieces of fried fish or cooked shrimp, fresh cheese slices, sweet potatoes or yams cut into small pieces, olives, and radishes
9. Scatter lettuce hearts as if they were bushes in a meadow

title: Cazuela (Peruvian Chicken Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat hen
- potatoes
- carrots
- turnips
- green beans
- rice, soaked
- good broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare a fat hen by plucking it very carefully, opening it, washing it repeatedly, as well as its offal, and sprinkling a little salt on it
2. Cut potatoes, carrots, and turnips into slices; mince green beans
3. Put all this with a handful of soaked rice in a pot or cooking vessel, along with a good quantity of good broth and the pieces and offal of the hen
4. Cover very well so the aroma does not escape and cook over good heat until the meat, on the hen's leg, falls apart when pressed
5. Then the cazuela is ready and is served

title: Cazuela Mejicana (Mexican-Style Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen, cut into small pieces
- pork, from chest or ribs
- ham with its bacon
- one sausage or chorizo
- a little beef
- potatoes
- onion
- tomato
- white cabbage hearts
- pork fat
- green chiles
- onion heads
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- oregano
- huacatay
- thyme
- bay leaf
- cooked potatoes
- fresh cheese
- egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces one good hen, pork from the chest or ribs, ham with its bacon, one sausage or chorizo, and a little beef
2. Cut potatoes, onion, tomato, and white cabbage hearts into small pieces
3. Fry green chiles and sliced onion heads in pork fat
4. Mix in the minced meats, salt, and pepper
5. When half fried, add two ladlefuls of broth and the vegetables, oregano, huacatay, thyme, and bay leaf
6. Bring to a boil, adding broth if it thickens
7. In the final boil, add cooked potatoes, well broken up, and strips of fresh cheese
8. Before serving, place at the bottom of the tureen a mixture of cooked egg yolks beaten and mixed with oil and vinegar

title: Chanchito (Roasted Suckling Pig)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- butter
- fine herbs
- onions
- clove spice
- pig liver
- fresh bacon
- mushrooms
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Kill the suckling pig in the same manner as a pig, but extract all the blood so the meat retains its natural whiteness.
2. Plunge it in boiling water for half a minute to remove the hair.
3. Extend it on the kitchen table and scrape it with a knife until it is clean.
4. Rub it with rough linen and wash it repeatedly.
5. Remove the legs and when cleaning the interior, leave the kidneys.
6. Fill the interior with a mixture of butter, finely chopped fine herbs, onions studded with clove spice, the cooked and chopped liver of the suckling pig mixed with fresh bacon and cooked mushrooms, seasoned with salt.
7. Secure the thighs with larding needles and spit it on the spit.
8. Once well heated and beginning to roast, sprinkle it three or four times with salt-seasoned water.
9. Then baste it with oil from time to time so the skin toasts well and crisps when chewed.
10. Serve very hot, straight from the spit to the table.
11. Serve various sauces separately according to the taste of the diners.

title: Chancho Enrollado (Rolled Pork)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four-month-old pork, cleaned and gutted
- garlic, ground separately
- pepper
- walnuts
- peanut
- almonds
- salt
- oregano
- good red wine, two bottles
- vinegar, two bottles
- water
- myrtle branch
- laurel leaves
- rosemary

## Directions

1. Debone the pork by starting with the ribs, loin, and then the remaining bones of legs and arms, leaving the head intact
2. Leave it in vinegar for twenty-four hours
3. Previously grind separately garlic, pepper, walnuts, peanut, almonds, salt and oregano
4. Sprinkle the necessary salt over the pork
5. Coat with ground garlic and sprinkle pepper
6. Spread the mass of peanut, walnuts and almonds over it
7. Roll it up tying with double twine from one end to the other
8. Tie the whole thing in a circle with the same twine
9. Place in a deep enameled porcelain pot or terra-cotta pot with two bottles of good red wine, two of vinegar, enough water to cover everything, and a bunch of myrtle, laurel leaves and rosemary
10. When a straw can be easily inserted into the meat, it is ready
11. Remove from fire and serve, or store after cooling it for several hours in a fresh, shady place

title: Chicha (Corn Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn flour, defatted
- Water, sufficient amount

## Directions

1. Cook defatted corn flour (that is, with the grain oil removed) in plenty of water.
2. As it cooks, the oil of the grain will separate and rise to the surface.
3. After each operation, remove two glasses of the broth and deposit them in a clay vessel, the same as the one in which the flour cooks.
4. For each one removed, add two jugs of hot water kept at hand.
5. When in the prolonged cooking and transfer of the liquid the flour has disappeared from the bottom of the pot, remove it from the fire.
6. Combine all the broths and let them cool.
7. Place in a clay pot or jar, cover very well, and leave to ferment.

title: Chicha de Garbanzos (Chickpea Fermented Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickpeas, well washed
- Water
- Molasses (chancaca), ground
- Purple beans, one dozen raw
- Cloves, one dozen

## Directions

1. Soak well-washed chickpeas in cold water for 24 hours
2. Drain the soaking water and grind in a mill or mortar, very well ground
3. Place to cook with plenty of water over high heat for three hours
4. Strain through thin, strong cloth, pressing to extract the bulk of the juice
5. Regrind the remaining bagasse and mix with the juice already strained, pass through the strainer again
6. Combine with the rest and place in an earthen vessel to ferment, sweetening it with ground molasses and adding with it one dozen raw purple beans and one dozen cloves
7. Fermentation reaches proper point in five days in summer and eight days in winter

title: Chicha de Jora (Fermented Corn Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow corn, quantity as needed
- Water
- Straw
- Cabbage leaves or large weeds

## Directions

1. Soak yellow corn in a clay vessel of sufficient capacity for three days
2. Drain the water and empty the corn into a basket to drain well
3. Place to germinate in open vessels and broken clay pots, on straw and covering the surface with cabbage leaves or large weeds
4. If the weather is cold, this germination takes fifteen days; if hot, eight days suffice
5. After this time, remove the sprouted corn and extend it in the sun on cloths to dry, turning it from one side to the other
6. The germinated corn, now called jora, is ground, pulverizing it in the mortar, or if in large quantity, in the mill
7. Dissolve this flour in sufficient water to form a clear broth, and soak for two hours
8. When this infusion has become clear, set aside one glass
9. Then boil all the infusion in a large clay vessel over good heat for three hours
10. While hot, pass through a fine strainer, squeezing forcefully so that the juice is extracted from the bagasse, and let the liquid cool
11. Pour the reserved infusion into the bottom of the clay jar or botija to serve as a base for the already strained liquid, which is placed there to ferment, adding one glass of sediment from fermented chicha to serve as yeast
12. Cover the mouth of the vessel with a cloth folded in four and tied firmly with a rope around its neck
13. Cover the vessel with a wool blanket for three days, after which, upon uncovering, a delicious beverage is found

title: Chicha Morada (Purple Corn Fermented Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Purple corn, kernels removed from the cob
- Purple corn cobs (maslos)
- Water, regular quantity
- Sour cherries (guindas)
- Pineapple rinds or ananas peels
- Lemons, two, cut in pieces
- Sugar
- Cinnamon sticks
- Whole cloves
- Pineapple slices
- Cherries, two to three dozen

## Directions

1. With the kernels removed, place purple corn with its cobs to cook in a regular amount of water, with sour cherries, pineapple or ananas rinds, and two lemons cut in pieces
2. Let cook until the corn bursts open like a flower
3. Remove from heat and strain through thin gauze
4. Let cool and season with sugar, pieces of cinnamon, whole cloves, pineapple slices and two or three dozen cherries
5. Place to ferment in glass or clay vessels for three days
6. After this time, this confection has become the most exquisite beverage of its kind
7. Serve by adding new seasoning of sugar and cinnamon

title: Chuletas a la Puneñita (Breaded Young Goat or Lamb Chops)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- young goat or lamb chops
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- milk
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Flatten the chops one by one with a meat mallet.
2. Moisten them with broth and season with salt and pepper.
3. Place them in a heat-resistant dish brushed with butter and put them in the oven without letting them cook completely.
4. Remove them and let them cool.
5. In boiling milk, melt butter and add flour dissolved in milk.
6. Add two egg yolks to this cream.
7. Without letting the cream cool, roll the chops in it and then in breadcrumbs.
8. Once cold, brush them with egg, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and fry at the moment of serving.

title: Chupe Limeño (Lima-style Chowder)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onions, minced
- garlic, ground
- oregano
- salt to taste
- water
- potatoes
- milk
- fresh cheese
- eggs
- fish without scales or bones
- two dried and toasted chiles

## Directions

1. Cook minced onions in butter with very little ground garlic, very little oregano, and salt to taste, frying the sauce well
2. Add a small cup of water; when boiling, add peeled potatoes until lightly cooked
3. Pour in the desired quantity of milk and bring to a boil
4. When the potatoes are well cooked, add a good piece of minced fresh cheese and break as many eggs as there are people at table
5. While the chowder cooks, fry pieces of fish without scales or bones and add to the chowder
6. Also add two dried and toasted chiles, which give it an exquisite flavor
7. If during boiling the milk reduces, add more, as the first condition of chowder is that it have much broth
8. This dish is served only at lunch

title: Churrascos (Gaucho Grilled Meat)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- quadrilateral piece of meat, three centimeters thick, from the sirloin or hip of beef or lamb
- salt
- live coals

## Directions

1. Cut the meat in a quadrilateral shape with three centimeters of thickness from the sirloin or hip of beef or lamb
2. Clean it of hides, nerves and fats
3. Wash it in cold water and dry it with care
4. Give it a very slight seasoning of salt
5. Strike the surface with a mortar
6. Extend it over a bed of well-fanned live coals
7. At the same time, prepare another bed of lively coals nearby
8. When the edges of the churrasco begin to pale, quickly turn it over and extend it on the raw side, hastening to remove from the other side the coals adhering to it
9. This short operation is enough for the churrasco to be ready
10. Serve without sauces, as sauce would remove the appetizing flavor given by the immediate contact with fire

title: Cocido de Gallina a la Mejicana (Mexican-Style Stewed Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A fat hen
- Fresh lettuce leaves
- Parsley
- Mint
- One onion head
- Salt
- Water
- Offal: gizzard, liver, and heart
- A piece of toasted bread crumbs
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare a fat hen by plucking it carefully, opening it and washing it several times with cold water, as well as its offal: gizzard, liver, and heart
2. Stuff the hen with a piece of toasted bread crumbs, sewing the opening closed with needle and thread
3. Place in a terra-cotta or enameled iron pot to cook in a long boil, accompanied by a bundle of fresh lettuce leaves, parsley, and mint leaves tied with thread so they don't scatter, and an onion head in the center
4. Add very little salt at first, increasing it as the broth reduces and needs water added, either a small cup or two
5. When the flesh of the hen's leg falls apart with pressure, the stew is ready
6. After a short rest, serve the broth in a large porcelain cup to enhance the liquid
7. Serve the hen whole to the diner so they can choose the pieces themselves
8. Accompany with a sauce made from the offal used for stuffing, which should be carefully extracted, ground with the toasted bread crumbs, and seasoned with oil, vinegar, and slices of the onion that cooked with the herbs in the broth

title: Coliflor a la Correntina (Cauliflower in the Style of Corrientes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- grated cheese
- one or two sauces

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower as if for white sauce.
2. Place it in a porcelain vessel and turn it out onto the serving dish, whose bottom is covered with a layer of grated cheese.
3. Place the dish on the stove with gentle heat underneath, and covering the surface of the cauliflower with another layer of grated cheese, cover it with its lid or with a sheet, and place hot coals and live embers on top so that the gentle heat on the bottom cooks without burning, and on the surface the cheese melts, adhering to the cauliflower.
4. Serve it accompanied by one or two sauces to the taste of the diners.

title: Conejo a la "Suma Guarmi" (Rabbit "Suma Guarmi" Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- vinegar
- salt
- butter
- aji (chili pepper)
- sticks
- buttered paper
- yellow aji sauce
- English mustard
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. After peeling, washing, and gutting the rabbit, give it a final bath in vinegar and salt.
2. Open it well, flattening the loin with blows from the pestle or any other weight.
3. Coat it in butter fried with aji, and after a light sprinkling of salt, cross two sticks lengthwise through the interior of the body to keep it open.
4. Wrap it in buttered paper and place it on the spit over a brisk fire, in the open air or in a spacious kitchen, so that it does not become impregnated with the smoke of other foods.
5. When the rabbit is cooked, remove the paper so that it takes on the golden color of perfect cooking.
6. Serve with yellow aji sauce for the creoles and English mustard for foreigners: both seasoned with a mixture of hard-boiled egg yolks dissolved in oil and vinegar, with chopped parsley.

title: Conejo a la Bella Monjita (Rabbit the Beautiful Little Nun Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- oregano
- cumin
- garlic
- butter
- bacon
- flour
- Porto wine or sweet wine
- small onions
- pickled gherkins
- olives
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit with all cleanliness requirements and cut it into four quarters
2. Place the quarters for one hour in an adobo of vinegar, oil, salt, pepper, oregano, a pinch of cumin and one crushed garlic clove
3. After removing from the adobo, dry it well with a napkin and fry it in butter together with small pieces of bacon the size and shape of dice
4. When the bacon has browned, remove it, leaving the rabbit to finish frying
5. Once the rabbit is fried, return the bacon to the pan; add two tablespoons of flour and mix it well to incorporate it
6. After one minute of boiling, pour in half a bottle of Porto wine or another sweet wine
7. Cook over high heat with seasoning of salt and pepper and four or five small onions until the sauce has reduced and thickened
8. Serve with pickled gherkins alternating with olives around the edges of the platter and sprigs of parsley on the surface

title: Conejo a la Challa (Rabbit Challa Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- salt
- chili pepper
- cumin
- pork lard
- poultry
- mushrooms
- garlic
- fresh bacon
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit as described and open it completely
2. Extend the rabbit face down and strike it three times from top to bottom along the spine with a pestle to stretch it well
3. Season with salt and, if desired, with a little chili pepper and ground cumin
4. Fry it in pork lard over high heat with a stone or other weight on the loin to flatten it so it fries better
5. Turn it from one side to the other two or three times, and when it has taken the golden color this type of frying requires, remove it from the fire and drain all the grease
6. Prepare a filling of chopped poultry, mushrooms, and one crushed garlic clove
7. Cover the bottom and sides of a baking dish with thin slices of fresh bacon
8. Spread half of the filling on top, place the rabbit on top, sprinkling it with pepper and grated nutmeg
9. Fill the dish with the rest of the filling, covering it all with a thin slice of bacon
10. Put the lid on and seal its edges with paste to prevent steam from escaping
11. Place it in the oven where it must remain for three hours
12. Serve cold

title: Corbina Rellena (Stuffed Corbina)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A tender corbina
- Water, two liters, boiling
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Tomato
- Garlic, one clove
- Parsley
- Butter
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Milk
- Pickled olives
- Pickled gherkins
- Pepper
- Cumin

## Directions

1. Choose a tender corbina: one can tell by the fact that it has not yet reached its natural size. After descaling it, opening it and washing it many times with cold water, hang it up, insert a small stick through its mouth to keep it open, and pour two liters of boiling water through it.
2. The water, running along the body from head to tail, shrinks the flesh, thus revealing all the spines, which are quickly removed.
3. Then wash it again and leave it for an hour in water saturated with salt and vinegar.
4. Meanwhile, mince onion and tomato, add one clove of garlic and parsley ground, and cook them in butter.
5. Mince finely hard-boiled eggs; soak bread crumbs in milk; and when the onion being fried takes on a golden color, add the minced eggs and the bread crumbs soaked in milk, add pickled olives and gherkins, sprinkle with pepper and cumin, add one tablespoon of vinegar, and stir, cooking for five minutes more, remove from heat and use this to stuff the corbina.
6. Once the corbina is stuffed, close the opening with stitches of needle, and well buttered, as well as the paper in which it is to be wrapped so that it toasts on both sides, place it in the oven.

title: Cordero Asado (Roasted Lamb)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb at mid-lactation stage
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- oregano
- cumin
- two onion heads
- two cloves of garlic
- lamb liver
- lamb tongue
- hard-boiled eggs
- two dozen toasted and ground peanut kernels
- almonds
- raisins
- olives
- butter
- pickled gherkins
- lettuce hearts
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Skin, open, and wash the lamb.
2. Place it in a marinade of vinegar, oil, salt, pepper, oregano, cumin, two onion heads, and two cloves of garlic, minced, for three hours.
3. While the lamb marinates, prepare the stuffing with minced cooked liver and tongue of the lamb; finely chopped hard-boiled eggs; two dozen toasted and ground peanut kernels; almonds, raisins, and olives.
4. Put all of this in a pan over the fire with a piece of butter, seasoned with pepper and salt.
5. Stir for five minutes to incorporate it, and remove from the fire.
6. Remove the lamb from the marinade, pat it dry, and stuff it with this mixture.
7. Sew it closed with a needle and strong thread.
8. Spread it with butter, and place it in a buttered pan.
9. Put it in a moderately hot oven to cook slowly.
10. Serve with pickled gherkins and lettuce hearts seasoned with oil and vinegar.
11. The head and legs of the lamb, which have also been marinated, should be cooked slowly and served in tomato sauce.

title: Cordero Asado (Roasted Lamb)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb at half nursing age
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- oregano
- cumin
- two heads of onion
- two cloves of garlic, ground

## Directions

1. Prepare a lamb at half nursing age
2. Skin it, open it and wash it
3. Place it for three hours in a marinade of vinegar, oil, salt, pepper, oregano, cumin, two heads of onion and two cloves of garlic ground

title: Cordial (Milk and Almond Drink)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter of good milk
- 3 eggs
- sugar
- almonds
- coconut
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 glass of good grape brandy or cognac

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter of good milk with 3 eggs and sweeten with toasted sugar
2. Grind almonds and coconut, dissolve with a little of the same milk, pass through a sieve
3. Pour on top 6 well-beaten egg yolks, one for every 3 cups
4. Beat everything very well with a chocolate whisk
5. Remove from fire at once
6. Add 1 glass of good grape brandy or, failing that, good cognac
7. Serve after beating well and making foam

title: Costillitas a la Chinga (Little Pork Ribs Prepared a Certain Way)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs, prepared and pounded
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs, grated
- Fine herbs, chopped
- Salt, pepper, and nutmeg
- Butter for wrapping
- English mustard sauce
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Broth

## Directions

1. After preparing and pounding the pork ribs, butter them and roll them in grated breadcrumbs mixed with chopped fine herbs, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
2. Wrap them in paper spread with butter and cook them in a roasting pan, turning them so the paper does not burn.
3. Serve with a spicy sauce made from English mustard thickened with hard-boiled egg yolks dissolved in broth.

title: Crema de Coco (Coconut Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice starch
- one large coconut, or four dozen Paraguay coconuts in its place
- milk
- one liter of milk
- one pound of sugar
- twelve egg yolks
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- sugar and cinnamon for dusting

## Directions

1. Soak half a pound of rice starch in cold milk. Grate a large coconut, or four dozen Paraguay coconuts if unavailable. Mix the grated coconut with a little milk, enough to moisten it and extract the juice, straining it through cheesecloth. The small amount of milk mixed with the coconut should be hot so that when squeezed through the strainer it yields its thickest juice. Grind and strain the remaining pulp again with another small amount of hot milk.
2. Bring one liter of milk with one pound of sugar to a boil. When it reaches its first boil, remove from heat and mix in vigorously the rice starch already dissolved in the milk that soaked it, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming. Once cooked and well thickened, remove from heat again and add twelve well-beaten egg yolks, stirring with great vigor, and return to heat once the yolks have been well incorporated into the milk. As soon as it begins to boil, remove from heat for the last time. Add the coconut milk, to which one teaspoon of cinnamon has been mixed, and stirring well to incorporate everything, pour into a flat dish. When cooled, dust thickly with sugar and cinnamon, and pass a heated iron over it to form caramel.

title: Crema de Frambuesas (Raspberry Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 grams powdered gum arabic
- 125 grams ground sugar
- 1/4 liter milk
- raspberries
- sponge cake biscuits

## Directions

1. Put the powdered gum arabic and ground sugar in a quarter liter of milk and let the gum and sugar dissolve
2. Grind raspberries and pass through a sieve, mixing this juice with the milk
3. Beat the milk strongly and collect the foam as it forms, converting it into cream
4. Arrange the cream in a pyramid on a dish, surrounded by sponge cake biscuits with their lower ends cut so they stand upright

title: Croquetas a lo Marino (Seafaring-Style Croquettes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken meat, roasted in oven, finely minced
- butter
- flour, one tablespoon
- milk, one glass
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- egg yolks, beaten
- bread crumbs
- hard-boiled eggs, halves
- black olives

## Directions

1. Mince finely the roasted oven chicken meat and fry in a pan with butter
2. When half fried, add one tablespoon of flour and stir constantly so it does not burn
3. Pour in one glass of milk and season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. When everything is cooked, incorporated, and in a mass, let it cool
5. Form balls the size of an egg, roll them in beaten egg yolks, then in bread crumbs
6. Fry in butter until golden
7. Place on a serving dish, alternating with halves of hard-boiled eggs and black olives

title: Croquetas de Fréjoles (Bean Croquettes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked beans, skins removed, ground
- Six eggs, beaten
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest scrapings
- Three tablespoons flour
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. Cook the beans, remove their skins, and grind them in a mortar.
2. Beat six eggs and mix them into the bean paste along with sugar, cinnamon, lemon zest scrapings, and three tablespoons of flour to make the mixture somewhat compact.
3. Heat a piece of butter in a pan over high heat until it boils.
4. Drop the paste by spoonfuls into the hot butter.
5. When one side is golden, flip to brown the other side and complete frying.
6. Serve hot.

title: Cuajada a la Balmoral (Balmoral Curdled Milk)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- good fresh milk, preferably the last milk from the cow's udder
- the purple part of 2 thistle flowers, ground fine
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare the thistle flower parts, finely ground in a mortar and enclosed in thin gauze
2. Milk the cow and use the milk while still warm with natural body heat
3. Put the milk in a deep dish
4. Stir the milk with the thistle flower bundle for two minutes, squeezing it and stirring in all directions
5. Let the milk rest after two minutes
6. Test the coagulation by moving the container; when the milk does not move when shaken slightly, the curdled milk is ready
7. Serve in bowls with a generous sprinkling of sugar and cinnamon
8. Alternatively, pour the milk into cups immediately after adding the thistle flower

title: Dorado a la Sevillana (Seville-Style Dorado)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- dorado (a large fish)
- salt
- vinegar
- flour
- lard
- oil
- pepper
- onion heads
- tomatoes
- parsley
- green chili peppers
- olives
- pickled vegetables
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Scale, open, and wash the dorado, then cut it into cross-sectional pieces five centimeters thick
2. Place the pieces in a wooden, terra-cotta, or porcelain-enameled vessel filled with salted water strongly saturated with vinegar for one hour
3. Coat the dorado pieces in flour and fry them in lard in an enameled pan
4. Cook the head in an identical pot with salted water saturated with vinegar
5. Arrange the fried pieces in a circle on a serving platter with the head in the center
6. Pour over the whole a sauce made of oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, sliced onion heads, chopped tomatoes and parsley, and strips of green chili peppers
7. Garnish with olives and pickled vegetables between sprigs of fine herbs

title: Duraznos Rellenos (Stuffed Peaches)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- water
- cooked meat, chopped
- hard-boiled eggs, chopped
- Corinth raisins
- white onion, finely chopped
- tomato juice
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- cumin
- beef broth, half cup

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches delicately, taking care not to remove flesh, and place them in a regular amount of water to boil.
2. Give them two short boils, remove from water and let them cool.
3. Cut a round piece from the side where the stem is, and pressing them and using the knife point, extract the pit through this opening.
4. Fill them with picadillo of cooked meat, chopped hard-boiled eggs, and Corinth raisins.
5. Prepare a sauce previously cooked and composed of finely chopped white onion, tomato juice, butter, pepper, salt, a pinch of cumin and half a cup of broth.
6. Place the stuffed peaches in an oven-safe dish with this sauce, taking care to close the filling by placing the small rounds removed to extract the pit.

title: Duraznos Rellenos (Stuffed Peaches)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- water
- ground cooked meat
- hard-boiled eggs, chopped
- Corinth raisins
- white onion, finely chopped
- tomato juice
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- cumin
- broth

## Directions

1. Boil the peaches in a regular quantity of water. Give them two short boils, remove from water and let cool.
2. Cut a small round piece from the side of the stem, and by pressing and using the tip of a knife, extract the pit, filling them with ground cooked meat, chopped hard-boiled eggs, and Corinth raisins.
3. In a heat-resistant dish, prepare a sauce made of finely chopped white onion, tomato juice, butter, pepper, salt, a pinch of cumin and half a cup of broth.
4. Place the stuffed peaches in the dish, taking care to close the filling with the small round pieces that were removed, and arrange them in a group, one next to the other, so they cook without accident.
5. Cover the dish and place coals on top, cook slowly over low heat for half an hour.
6. Serve in the same dish on a platter.

title: El Cafe (Coffee)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee, ground and sifted twice
- Water, cold
- Water, hot

## Directions

1. Half an hour before serving, with the coffee already between the first and second sifting, add three spoonfuls of cold water, intended to moisten it and prepare the aroma for the second emission of boiling water, which ruins it when falling on dry powder
2. Next, pour the hot water slowly and in three emissions, to allow the aroma to exhale

title: El Chocolate (Chocolate Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce of chocolate, previously grated, per cup
- water or milk

## Directions

1. Place one ounce of grated chocolate in each cup and fill it with the water or milk in which it is to be made
2. Pour into the chocolate pot and place over fire, stirring constantly with the whisk to mix it
3. When it begins to boil, remove from fire; beat for two minutes, and return to fire, beating and stirring always
4. When with the second boil it has risen to the edge of the container, remove from fire again; beat for another two minutes and return to fire again
5. When, rising further, the third boil occurs, remove from fire, beat and serve if possible in cups similar to jicaras which preserve its heat and creamy foam

title: El Chocolate (Chocolate)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Chocolate, previously grated, one ounce per cup
- Water or milk

## Directions

1. Place one ounce of previously grated chocolate in each cup and fill it with the water or milk in which it is to be made
2. Pour into the chocolate pot and place on the fire, stirring constantly with the beater to mix it

title: El Hallazgo de Elenitta (Elenitta's Discovery)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of wheat flour
- a glass of good red wine, either Burgundy or Priorat
- three egg yolks
- sugar to moderately sweeten the paste
- a small cup of fresh melted butter
- flour for dusting hands
- high-point syrup
- Burgundy red wine
- powdered cinnamon and cloves
- almonds
- raisins
- walnuts
- pine nuts
- toasted sesame

## Directions

1. Make a mound with half a pound of wheat flour with a hollow in the center
2. Into the hollow, add a glass of good red wine (either Burgundy or Priorat), three egg yolks, sugar to moderately sweeten the paste, and a small cup of fresh melted butter
3. Knead this paste by beating it with your hands, dusting them with flour so the paste does not stick
4. When the paste is united and smooth, extend it with a rolling pin until it is very thin, and cut it into circles the size of the plate in which the filling is to be made
5. Butter the bottom and edges of the plate, and also butter the dough circles
6. Arrange three circles of dough, one on top of the other, in the bottom of the plate
7. Spread the filling on top, composed off the heat with high-point syrup, Burgundy red wine, powdered cinnamon and cloves, almonds, raisins, walnuts, pine nuts, and toasted sesame
8. Decorate the filling with additional raisins, almonds, and strips of candied citron
9. Cover with three more layers of dough, not as thin as those on the bottom
10. Wet the edges around the border to seal both layers of dough and crimp them
11. Form a cord from the same dough by rounding and stretching it between your hands, and make decorative relief designs on the surface with it
12. Brush with a coat of butter and place in the oven
13. When cooked and still hot, dust with sugar

title: El Mate (Mate Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- yerba mate
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil water with three boilings in a clay or enameled iron kettle
2. In the mate already filled with yerba alone, and when the water gives its first boil, pour two spoonfuls of cold water through the bombilla
3. Press without stirring, and let the yerba become moistened, so that the action of cold water draws out its aroma
4. When the water from the kettle has given three boils, extract from the mate the cold water that remains after moistening the yerba by carefully tilting the mate and pressing it on the other side with the bombilla
5. Add sugar, and after this pour the hot water calmly
6. When serving, move the bombilla around but without lifting it

title: Embozo a la Elvirita (Elvirita's Pastry)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- una libra de harina (one pound of flour)
- media libra de grasa de vaca, chancho, o mantequilla (half pound beef fat, pork fat, or butter)
- sal (salt)
- azúcar (sugar)
- leche (milk)
- carne de solomo (sirloin meat)
- pasas de uva (raisins)
- huevos duros (hard boiled eggs)
- aceitunas (olives)
- pimienta en polvo (powdered pepper)
- ajo molido (ground garlic)

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour, half pound of beef fat, pork fat, or butter; sufficient salt to taste, and equal quantity of sugar
2. Stir all together and add milk little by little, kneading with both hands to incorporate
3. Make a mixture of sirloin meat, raisins, chopped hard boiled eggs, olive pieces, powdered pepper, sufficient salt and sugar to taste, and a small pinch of ground garlic if desired
4. Extend the mass with a rolling pin and make the pastry, either in the form of a cake or empanada
5. Bake in an oven with strong heat for quick cooking

title: Embozo de Papas (Potato Croquettes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- papas (potatoes)
- cuatro huevos batidos (four beaten eggs)
- mantequilla (butter)
- sal (salt)
- harina (flour)
- especias (spices)
- cebolla (onion)
- tomate (tomato)
- perejil (parsley)
- carne cocida (cooked meat)
- almendras (almonds)
- pasas de uva (raisins)
- maní tostado y molido (roasted ground peanuts)
- huevos duros (hard boiled eggs)

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes unpeeled, then clean and grind them, making a mass
2. Add four beaten eggs, butter, and salt in necessary measure
3. Mix well. If the mass is loose, add flour bit by bit until it reaches sufficient consistency
4. Meanwhile, cook in butter conditioned with spices, onion, tomato, parsley, and finely chopped cooked meat
5. Add almonds, raisins, roasted ground peanuts, and hard boiled eggs sliced
6. Stir constantly, and when the onion is cooked, remove from fire
7. Cut the potato mass into proportional pieces, extend slightly, place sauce in the center with a spoon, wrap with the mass, and give it the enlarged shape of a potato, being careful to flour the hands well to prevent sticking
8. Fry in butter, turning from one side to the other so they brown evenly
9. Serve without sauce, with parsley sprigs and mint leaves as garnish

title: Embrollo Diplomatico (Diplomatic Tangle)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen egg yolks
- two egg whites, well beaten
- a large glass of milk
- a glass of Port wine
- zest of the skin of a lemon
- cinnamon
- sugar
- a small piece of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix and beat all ingredients together
2. Pour into a buttered mold
3. Bake in the oven

title: Embuchado Blanco (White Sausage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- onions, twenty, cut into dice
- butter, half a pound
- chicken fat and roasted oven chicken meat
- bread, equal quantity to the chicken, well soaked in cream and then squeezed
- egg yolks, six raw
- salt
- pepper, whole grain and ground
- pure cream, one glass
- sheep intestines, previously inflated and dried

## Directions

1. Cut twenty onions into dice and after washing in boiling water, cook in a pan with half a pound of butter
2. Mince and grind together the chicken fat and roasted oven chicken meat with an equal quantity of bread that has been well soaked in cream and then squeezed
3. Dissolve everything with six raw egg yolks, salt, whole and ground pepper, and one glass of pure cream
4. Add all of this to the pan in which the onions are frying
5. After one minute of boiling, stirring to mix the ingredients, remove from heat and let cool
6. Once cooled, fill the previously inflated and dried sheep intestines with this mixture using the same procedure as for making sausages

title: Empanada de Fiambre (Cold Meat Empanada)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Bread yeast
- Hot salted water
- Lard from fried bacon
- Ham, sliced thin
- Black olives, pitted

## Directions

1. Form the flour into a mound on a table and make a hollow in the center.
2. Dissolve a piece of bread yeast in hot lightly salted water and pour it little by little into the hollow while stirring, forming a stiff dough.
3. Knead the dough, gradually adding lard extracted from fried bacon, until it becomes soft and smooth.
4. Cover with a folded cloth and let it rest for one hour.
5. Divide into equal portions and dust with flour above and below, then stretch with a rolling pin into thin wheels of desired size on a cloth.
6. Cut thin slices of ham with a very sharp knife.
7. Place two ham slices separated by a layer of pitted black olives on half of each wheel of dough.
8. Fold the dough over the filling and seal the edges with a crimp.
9. Place on baking sheets in the oven and remove when golden brown, cooking quickly.

title: Empanada de Fiambre (Cold Meat Empanada)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- bread yeast
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the flour necessary for the quantity of empanadas to be made, and form it into a mound on a table. Make a hollow in the center.
