2. Dissolve a small piece of bread yeast in hot, lightly salted water, and with this water, pouring it little by little into the central hollow of the mound of flour, stirring it, a stiff dough is formed, which as it is made is kneaded until...

title: Emparedados a la Rosarina (Rosario-Style Sandwiches)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cold bread
- milk
- 1 chicken
- ham
- pepper
- flour
- 1 clove garlic
- butter
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After scraping the crust from cold bread with a knife, cut it into slices that are soaked in milk.
2. Cook a chicken, cut it into pieces, and chop its meat very well with a piece of ham. Season with pepper, flour and 1 clove of ground garlic. Stir and mix, fry in butter.
3. Beat egg yolks, coat the bread slices with them, and fry them well browned.
4. Then, between two of these fried slices place the chicken and ham filling, coat again in egg, and fry.
5. Serve hot at lunch; and as a cold dish it is very pleasant.

title: Ensalada a la Rosa de Córdoba (Salad in the Style of the Rose of Córdoba)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- thick slices of white onion heads
- hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- French mustard

## Directions

1. Cut onion heads into thick slices and cook them with a boil of half an hour.
2. Drain the water so they remain dry and can be removed from the pot without losing their shape.
3. Arrange them in layers in the salad bowl, alternating with hard-boiled eggs cut in half.
4. Pour over everything by spoonfuls a mixture of oil and vinegar beaten with pepper and a spoonful of French mustard.

title: Ensalada de Acelgas (Chard Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- chard
- water
- salt
- onion heads
- broth
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- bread

## Directions

1. Wash the chard and boil it in plenty of water without salt, with two onion heads.
2. When the chard is so cooked that it falls apart at the touch, remove it from the fire, drain it, and after removing the stems, break it apart very well with a spoon until it is reduced to a paste, which is thinned by adding one tablespoon of broth.
3. Chop the onion that has been cooked finely, mix it with the chard, and season it as a salad with the oil, vinegar, and pepper, which should have been previously beaten in a cup. It should be noted that this salad requires more oil than vinegar.
4. Toast very thin slices of bread on the grill, and on each one place one tablespoon of chard, arranging them with good taste in a small salad bowl.

title: Ensalada de Coliflor (Cauliflower Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- water
- onion
- parsley
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the cauliflower well and boil it with much water that covers it entirely in a pot.
2. When the foam subsides, remove it from the fire; drain the water, and without removing it from the pot so that it does not lose its shape, wash it in water that is warmer than lukewarm so that it does not harden.
3. Extract it carefully from the pot and cut off the lower branches.
4. Chop these branches very finely, together with one onion head and a bunch of parsley.
5. Mix, beating together, the necessary oil and vinegar with pepper and salt; pour part of it over the chopped mixture, which is stirred and arranged on the bottom of the salad bowl.
6. Next, place the cauliflower in the center, taking care to plant it well, and bathe it with the rest of the oil and vinegar mixture.

title: Ensalada de Lechuga (Lettuce Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- black olives
- onions

## Directions

1. Chop the outer leaves very finely, leaving the hearts intact, which are arranged forming a small grove in the center of the salad bowl.
2. Beat together one cup of vinegar and four tablespoons of good oil with a sprinkling of pepper and salt until completely mixed.
3. Place the chopped lettuce mixed in this mixture in the salad bowl with its arrangement of hearts.
4. Top with black olives and, if desired, onions cut in thin slices.

title: Ensalada de Paltas (Avocado Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- avocados at their first sign of ripeness
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- tender lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Select avocados at their first sign of ripeness.
2. Remove the skin and cut them into thin vertical slices.
3. Arrange them in layers in the salad bowl, pouring over each layer by spoonfuls the oil and vinegar beaten with salt, followed by a light sprinkling of pepper.
4. If to be served at a table, place an avocado at the center as a garnish, over tender lettuce leaves.

title: Ensalada de Papas (Potato Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- garlic

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes, remove their skin, wash them, and cut them into slices, alternating with hard-boiled eggs cut in the same manner.
2. Season with the proper oil, vinegar, and salt, previously beaten together with pepper, one roasted onion head and one roasted garlic clove, washed and ground in the mortar.

title: Ensalada de Remolachas (Beet Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- vinegar
- water
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- pickled gherkins
- green olives

## Directions

1. Cook the beets, cut them into thin slices, and soak them in a mixture of vinegar and water for two hours.
2. Drain the water and vinegar, leaving them dry, and pour over them the necessary oil and vinegar, which should have been previously beaten together with a sprinkling of pepper and salt.
3. Incorporate everything and arrange in the salad bowl with an edging of pickled gherkins and green olives.

title: Ensalada Polonesa (Polish Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- tender lettuce hearts
- cooked beef
- cooked potatoes
- onion
- parsley
- roasted garlic clove
- vinegar
- pure olive oil
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley branches
- black olives

## Directions

1. Chop very finely the tender lettuce hearts, cooked beef, cooked potatoes, onion, and parsley.
2. Add one roasted garlic clove, well ground.
3. Incorporate all of this and season with the necessary good vinegar, pure olive oil, pepper, salt, and a light sprinkling of nutmeg.
4. Arrange in the salad bowl with an edging of parsley branches and black olives.

title: Envueltos a la Laurita (Laurita's Rolls)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- good sirloin veal, one piece
- bread crumbs, one handful
- fresh bacon
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- beef marrow
- egg yolks, beaten, with one egg white
- bread crumbs, grated
- canned or marinated onions and green chiles
- tomato juice sauce

## Directions

1. Choose a good piece of sirloin veal and cut it crosswise into thin slices, which are then flattened with a mallet
2. Grind in a mortar one handful of bread crumbs, fresh bacon, parsley, salt, pepper, and beef marrow
3. Spread two tablespoons of this mixture on each slice of veal and cover with another prepared slice
4. Wrap in a beaten mixture of egg yolks with one egg white, roll in bread crumbs
5. Toast on a grill over moderate heat
6. Serve with relief of canned or marinated onions and green chiles, and separately accompanied with tomato juice sauce

title: Espuma de Mar (Sea Foam)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- milk to fill a serving dish
- ground almonds and coconut, dissolved in milk
- cloves
- cinnamon
- anise
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Sweeten the quantity of milk necessary to fill a serving dish to taste
2. Season the milk with ground almonds and coconut, dissolved in milk and passed through a sieve
3. Heat to a boil, adding at the same time cloves, cinnamon, and anise enclosed in a piece of gauze tied with string
4. Once it boils, remove from the heat and immediately pour into the serving dish
5. Add six egg whites that have been beaten to stiff peaks, having them ready beforehand
6. Stir quickly, but only with two turns of the spoon, and let the foam that forms in the moment reappear
7. Let it cool and serve

title: Espárragos a la Colegiala (Asparagus in the Style of the Schoolgirl)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper
- cream puree

## Directions

1. Lightly boil the asparagus in salted water without cooking them too much, to preserve their most delicate part.
2. Tie them in small bundles that represent one serving.
3. Marinate them for two hours in a mixture of vinegar and oil seasoned with salt and pepper.
4. When serving, transfer them to a dish of cream puree.

title: Espárragos a la Crema (Asparagus in Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- boiling water
- fresh butter
- cream or good milk
- one spoonful of flour
- salt
- pepper
- three egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Submerge the asparagus in boiling water and place them in a pan over low heat with very fresh butter.
2. Sauté them in the butter without allowing them to brown.
3. Add to this sauté one cup of cream or good milk.
4. Add one spoonful of flour dissolved in milk, salt and pepper, and cook them until the sauce is well reduced.
5. At the moment of serving, remove the pan from the heat and complete the sauce by pouring in three beaten egg yolks, stirring them in to incorporate.
6. Serve accompanied by a mixture of oil, vinegar, salt and pepper.

title: Estofado Arequipeño (Arequipa Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of the best beef
- Lamb loin
- Half a chicken
- A slice of bacon
- A chorizo
- Onion heads cut in half
- A sprig of parsley
- A handful of soaked chickpeas
- A large piece of butter
- Two cloves of garlic
- Ground and whole black pepper
- One ounce of chocolate
- One bottle of red wine
- Half a bottle of water
- Six tablespoons of good vinegar
- Salt in small quantity
- A little cumin

## Directions

1. Cut all the meat into large pieces and arrange in the pot
2. Add the onion heads, parsley, chickpeas, butter, garlic, pepper, chocolate, wine, water, vinegar, salt, and cumin
3. Cover the pot well and bring to a boil over low heat for four hours without uncovering it
4. Shake the pot from time to time to prevent it from sticking to the bottom
5. When ready to serve, uncover and enjoy the delicious aroma

title: Estofado de Corbina (Braised Corbina)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Corbina (white fish), cut into regular pieces and washed repeatedly in salted water
- Butter
- Onion, sliced into rounds
- Pepper
- Roasted tomatoes, seeded, peeled and ground
- Garlic, one clove, ground
- Oregano, ground
- Good vinegar, half a bottle
- Water, half a bottle
- Pickled gherkins
- Black olives

## Directions

1. On the bottom of an enameled pot, place over a layer of butter another layer of onion slices with a light dusting of pepper.
2. Over this place a layer of corbina chunks with another light, very light dusting of pepper, and continue thus until done.
3. Once done, spread over all a sauce of roasted tomatoes that have been seeded, peeled and ground, onion slices, pepper, one clove of garlic ground, oregano likewise ground, and half a bottle of good vinegar and half of water.
4. Set to cook over low heat. When the onion is well cooked, the braised dish is ready.
5. Serve with garnishes of pickled gherkins and black olives.

title: Estofado de Tres Carnes (Three-Meat Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef from the chest
- a piece of pork
- a hen
- a leg of mutton or pork, preferably the latter
- fresh bacon without salt
- a bouquet of aromatic herbs composed of parsley, basil, mint or lemon balm, rosemary and bay leaf, around green onion leaves
- two cloves of garlic, chopped
- two onion heads
- two dozen Malaga raisins
- two dozen almonds
- peppercorns
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- a pinch of cumin
- a tablespoon of salt
- a tablespoon of sugar
- two ounces of pork fat from fresh bacon, fried with paprika
- a quarter of legitimate priorato wine, lightly saturated with water

## Directions

1. Prepare a piece of beef from the chest, a piece of pork, and a hen; add if desired a leg of mutton or pork, preferring the latter
2. After properly washing with lukewarm water, place them in an enameled iron pot lined with slices of fresh unsalted bacon
3. Between the meat place a bouquet of aromatic herbs composed of parsley, basil, mint or lemon balm, rosemary and bay leaf, all around green onion leaves; tie the bouquet with string to prevent it from scattering
4. Place also between the meat two chopped cloves of garlic and two onion heads
5. Add two dozen Malaga raisins, two dozen almonds, some peppercorns, a piece of whole cinnamon, a pinch of cumin, a tablespoon of salt, a tablespoon of sugar and two ounces of pork fat extracted from fresh bacon, fried with paprika to give it color
6. Pour over all this a quarter of legitimate priorato wine, lightly saturated with water
7. Seal the pot hermetically and place it on a strong fire for the first boil, then for five hours at the coals over a low fire, being careful not to open it; instead only remove it frequently from the fire and without opening it, stir it to prevent it from sticking to the bottom
8. When serving, take care to remove the pieces with two forks to prevent them from falling apart
9. After placing the meat and hen in the center, pour the sauce over them circularly and place on the surface any garnishes desired, whether slices of eggs or pickled gherkins

title: Fiambre a la Stanley (Stanley-Style Cold Dish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- fat chicken
- ham slices
- sausage pieces, fried
- hard-boiled eggs, halves
- olives
- beef broth, one liter
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Stuff a fat chicken with thin slices of ham, pieces of fried sausage, halves of hard-boiled eggs, and olives
2. Cook in one liter of beef broth at a rolling boil for one hour
3. Let cool and drain
4. Wrap in puff pastry and roast in the oven
5. This dish is not only a cold dish but also figures as an exquisite morsel when served hot, accompanied separately with parsley or tomato sauce

title: Friolera a la Azucena (Azucena's Delicacy)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- six tablespoons of flour
- one cup of heavy cream
- a pinch of salt
- six tablespoons of powdered sugar
- a dozen cherries, washed but with their pits
- cinnamon
- butter
- sugar and cinnamon for serving

## Directions

1. Break three eggs into a bowl and add six tablespoons of flour, dissolving it with one cup of heavy cream to form a clear batter. Add a pinch of salt, six tablespoons of powdered sugar, and a dozen well-washed cherries with their pits still intact. Well mixed and dusted with cinnamon, arrange in a buttered baking tin with edges, and place in a well-heated oven after scattering small pieces of butter over the surface of the batter.
2. Serve hot with a dusting of sugar and cinnamon.

title: Fréjoles o Porotos a la Limeña (Lima-Style Beans)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried beans
- Onion head
- Fresh bacon without salt, a piece
- Pork fat, fresh, half a pound
- Onion heads, 2, finely chopped
- Garlic cloves, 2, ground
- Salt

## Directions

1. Soak the dried beans in cold water for twenty-four hours, by which time the skin will have loosened; remove it carefully.
2. Wash them very well and place them to cook in cold water with an onion head and a piece of fresh bacon without salt.
3. Heat half a pound of fresh pork fat together with two finely chopped onion heads, two ground garlic cloves, and salt; when this sauce is golden, add the beans which should be almost falling apart, as they must be cooked for at least three hours.
4. Stir well until they come to a boil again, and stirring constantly, let them fall apart completely and reach the smoothness and consistency of a purée.
5. Cut the bacon into pieces and add it to the beans.

title: Gallina a la Persa (Persian-Style Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One fat chicken
- Sweet, juicy and fragrant apples
- Bread
- Butter
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare a fat chicken by killing it the day before
2. When emptying it, be careful not to remove the interior fat
3. Choose very sweet, juicy and fragrant apples; chop them finely, mix them with very finely chopped bread pieces, and fry them in butter
4. During frying, add a little sugar, very little, just enough to increase the sweetness of the apple
5. When this has cooked well, stuff the chicken, coat it with butter and roast it for a long time, frequently removing it from the fire to baste it with its own juice, alternating with coatings of butter

title: Gallina a la Tucumanita (Tucuman-Style Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A hen
- Blood from the hen
- Salt
- Onions
- Tomatoes
- Bacon
- Chorizo
- Offal
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Pepper
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Butter
- Broth
- One dozen peanuts
- One dozen sweet almonds
- Bread crumb

## Directions

1. Cut the neck of the hen and catch the blood in a cup
2. Pluck, open, wash, and portion the hen, cooking it with a little salt
3. Make a seasoning with onions, tomatoes, bacon, and chorizo, which will cook with the hen, along with the offal and hard-boiled eggs: chop everything very finely and season with pepper, oregano, a pinch of cumin, and three tablespoons of butter
4. Pour part of the broth over this seasoning and add the hen pieces, cooking over low heat for half an hour
5. Meanwhile, toast and grind one dozen peanuts and the same number of sweet almonds
6. Grind with them a piece of bread crumb soaked in liquid and the blood that was collected
7. Mix together

title: Gallina en Sangre (Chicken in Blood Sauce)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Salt
- Onions
- Tomatoes
- Bacon
- Chorizo
- Chicken giblets
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Pepper
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Three tablespoons butter
- One dozen peanuts
- One dozen sweet almonds
- Piece of bread crumb soaked
- Chicken blood

## Directions

1. Cut the neck of the chicken and collect the blood in a cup
2. Pluck, open, wash and butcher the chicken and cook with a little salt
3. Make a dressing with onions, tomatoes, bacon and chorizo, which will have cooked with the chicken, as well as the giblets and hard-boiled eggs: everything finely chopped and seasoned with pepper, oregano, a pinch of cumin and three tablespoons of butter
4. Pour part of the broth over this dressing and add the chicken pieces, and cook over low heat for half an hour
5. Meanwhile, toast and grind one dozen peanuts and as many sweet almonds; grind with them a piece of soaked bread crumb and the blood that has been collected
6. Mix all this together, and a short time before removing the pot from the fire, add this final dressing; stir with the spoon, let it come to a half boil for a moment, and serve

title: Gallina Fiambre (Cold Prepared Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat chickens
- Salt
- Two whole onion heads
- Two dozen peppercorns
- Bundle of fine herbs
- String
- Chicken giblets
- Chicken gizzard
- Chicken liver
- Chicken heart
- Chicken tripes
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Four hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Kill two fat chickens
2. Wash and clean them well, inside and outside, and place them in a terra cotta pot or enameled iron pot, well submerged in water lightly seasoned with salt, together with two whole onion heads, two dozen peppercorns, and a bundle of fine herbs wrapped in string and pressed when throwing into the pot
3. Boil this cooking over high heat for three hours, adding boiling water little by little as it diminishes with prolonged cooking
4. The giblets, gizzard, liver and heart should cook together with the chickens
5. Clean the tripes, empty them, wash in three waters and cut into four centimeter pieces; once well cleaned inside, which will have been stirred, coat them with a beaten mixture of oil, vinegar, pepper and salt, and stirred again, roast them on the grill
6. When the meat of the leg or breast yields to finger pressure, the chicken is ready
7. Remove them from the pot and stuff them, adding to their giblets four hard-boiled eggs
8. The broth from this cooking is excellent, especially for children

title: Gelatina (Gelatin)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 beef feet, peeled, hooves removed and washed
- 1 fat hen, prepared
- water to cover
- 6 eggs
- 1 glass of good sherry wine
- 1 ounce of powdered cinnamon
- sugar to taste
- juice of 2 sweet oranges
- a few drops of lemon essence

## Directions

1. Cook the beef feet and hen in enough water to cover them for twenty-four hours, being careful to remove fat and add water as the broth reduces
2. When the meat separates from the bones, remove from heat, let cool slightly, and strain through a cloth, pressing to extract the juice
3. Mix the juice with 6 beaten eggs (yolks and whites together), 1 glass of sherry wine, 1 ounce of powdered cinnamon, sugar to taste, juice of 2 sweet oranges, and a few drops of lemon essence
4. Return to the heat and cook slowly for one hour until reduced to about 2 liters
5. Place a piece of bombazine cloth suspended over a receiving vessel to serve as a filter, and pour the hot juice through it by spoonfuls, allowing the egg whites and cinnamon to settle on top so the juice passes through drop by drop and comes out well clarified
6. Place the vessels containing the gelatin in the shade and open air for several hours to gel and set

title: Gelatina a la Romana (Roman Gelatin)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lamb feet, cleaned with care
- 2 veal feet, cleaned with care
- water to cover
- 1 cup of bitter almond milk (made from peeled, washed bitter almonds ground very finely, mixed with a little water and strained through gauze)
- sugar to taste
- 4 drops of heliotrope essence

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb and veal feet in sufficient water to cover them, skimming and degreasing carefully and thoroughly until they fall apart
2. The gelatin is ready to set at this point
3. Strain through a fine cloth
4. Mix in 1 cup of bitter almond milk
5. Add sugar to taste and 4 drops of heliotrope essence
6. Stir to incorporate everything thoroughly
7. Pour into cups or glasses to serve

title: Gelatina de Membrillo (Quince Gelatin)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ripe, healthy quinces for cooking
- 12 raw quinces
- water

## Directions

1. Cook 12 ripe, healthy quinces
2. Remove the seeds from 12 raw quinces with their surrounding gum and soak them in a cup with a little hot water
3. When the cooked quinces begin to fall apart and the cooking water has reduced, remove from heat and let cool enough to handle
4. Extract the seeds from the cooked quinces
5. Make a syrup from the cooking water and strain it through cloth
6. Rub the soaked seeds to extract all their gum and strain it well
7. Mix the seed gum with the syrup
8. Return to heat and cook until the point of coagulation is reached, which is known when the bubbles of the boil crack as they burst
9. Serve in cups

title: Gelatina de Naranja (Orange Gelatin)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges for juice
- orange peels
- sugar for syrup
- isinglass or Japanese gelatin leaf

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from oranges through a silk sieve
2. Make a syrup by boiling orange peels in water
3. Strain the syrup
4. Add the isinglass or Japanese gelatin leaf and the orange juice
5. Prepare the gelatin and pour it into orange peel halves cut crosswise
6. Surround with sugar

title: Golosina a la Ricardo (Ricardo's Sweet)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of sweet almonds
- a dozen bitter almonds
- 1 tablespoon of sugar
- zest of the peel of 1 lemon
- half pound of powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel, wash and clean the almonds
2. Grind them finely in a marble mortar, frequently adding 1 tablespoon of sugar to prevent the almond paste from becoming oily
3. When the paste is very fine, add the zest of lemon peel, half pound of powdered sugar, and 6 egg yolks
4. Mix everything by beating for half an hour
5. Beat 6 egg whites separately and well
6. Add them at the moment of placing the paste in a mold for the oven, which should be at moderate heat

title: Guisado de Aceitunas a la Lila (Olive Stew à la Lila)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- black olives, the meatiest
- butter
- three onion heads
- parsley
- capers
- flour
- good broth
- pepper
- salt
- one glass of Champagne wine
- pure oil

## Directions

1. Soak the black olives in warm water for half an hour
2. Wash them also with warm water and pit them carefully, keeping them whole
3. Make a sauce with three onion heads, parsley, and capers very finely minced and cooked in butter
4. When the onion begins to brown, sprinkle and stir in one tablespoon of flour, then three tablespoons of good broth to which pepper and salt have been added
5. Pour in one glass of Champagne wine
6. A moment later, mix in the olives
7. Let it boil for five minutes
8. When transferring to the serving dish, add a few drops of pure oil

title: Habas Rehogadas (Braised Fava Beans)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork fat
- pieces of bacon
- garlic
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- fava beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Put pork fat in a pan and heat it.
2. Add pieces of bacon, garlic, onion and parsley in good quantity.
3. When this is sautéed, add a sprinkling of pepper and then the fava beans and sufficient salt.
4. Let them cook slowly for one hour with the pan covered with a deep plate.

title: Habas Rehogadas (Sautéed Fava Beans)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork fat
- pieces of bacon
- garlic
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- fava beans
- salt
- bay leaf
- oregano
- mint
- thyme
- ham
- blood sausage
- half a small cup of anise liqueur

## Directions

1. Put pork fat in a pot. When it is hot, add pieces of bacon, garlic, onion, and parsley in good quantity.
2. When this is sautéed, add a sprinkling of pepper; then add the fava beans and sufficient salt.
3. Let them cook on low heat for one hour, with the pot covered by a deep plate in which you will put water, a bay leaf, oregano, mint, and thyme. The plate itself should be covered, and from time to time drops of the water it contains should be added to the stew.
4. After one hour of cooking, add ham and blood sausage cut into small pieces, and half a small cup of anise liqueur.
5. Let it season thus for half an hour, and serve. This is an extremely appetizing dish.

title: Habichuelas al Mondongo (Beans with Tripe)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tripe and intestines
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- onion
- tomato
- garlic
- oregano
- pepper
- lettuce hearts
- radishes

## Directions

1. Well washed, scraped and prepared, cook the pork tripe and intestines in lightly salted water
2. Once cooked, chop them finely and place them for two hours in an adobo of oil and vinegar, onion, tomato, garlic, oregano, salt and pepper
3. Serve with lettuce hearts basted in oil and vinegar seasoned with salt and pepper, alternating with radishes cut in quarters

title: Helado de Cafe (Coffee Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- two liters of good milk
- one cup of natural cream
- half pound of coffee, carefully roasted and ground

## Directions

1. Mix two liters of good milk with one cup of natural cream
2. Bring to a boil for ten minutes with half a pound of coffee that has been carefully roasted, ground, and enclosed in a fairly large flannel bag so the coffee can soak into the liquid through the fabric
3. Remove the milk from the fire and squeeze the flannel bag well three or four times after submerging it again
4. Empty the milk into another container
5. Sweeten strongly and allow to cool
6. Pass to an ice cream maker to freeze

title: Helado de Canela (Cinnamon Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Good cinnamon pieces, well broken
- Water, necessary quantity for the ice cream
- One ounce of ground cinnamon
- Sugar
- A few drops of cinnamon essence

## Directions

1. Cook pieces of good cinnamon, well broken, for one hour in the quantity of water necessary for the ice cream.
2. Add one ounce of ground cinnamon, enclosed in a small bag of flannel or gauze.
3. Remove from the fire and strain through a sieve.
4. Add sugar to taste.
5. When placing it to freeze, add a few drops of cinnamon essence to increase its perfume.

title: Helado de Crema (Cream Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of rice flour
- six very fresh egg yolks
- sixteen spoonfuls of powdered sugar
- one liter of cream
- one cup of coconut milk
- one cup of almond milk

## Directions

1. Dissolve one spoonful of rice flour in six very fresh egg yolks
2. Add sixteen spoonfuls of powdered sugar and one liter of cream to this mixture, stirring vigorously
3. Place this preparation in a pot and cook over low heat for a quarter hour, stirring constantly for half an hour with a wooden spoon
4. Once cooking is complete, allow the cream to cool
5. Add one cup of coconut milk and one cup of almond milk, both extracted by grinding in a mortar, diluted in a little milk, and strained through a strong cloth sieve that allows pressing and extracting all the juice
6. Stir well to incorporate the milk into the cream
7. Place in an ice cream maker to freeze

title: Helado de Espuma (Foam Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Good milk
- Cream, one cup
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Straw or ice cream paddle

## Directions

1. At five in the morning, fill two tin or zinc cans halfway with milk.
2. Wrap them in sheepskins very thoroughly soaked in water strongly seasoned with saltpeter, or lacking that, salt.
3. Place them on the back of a horse and make it trot one league, and bring it back at the same trot.
4. The milk, which must be carefully covered with a well-adjusted can lid and loose in its container, will shake like the sea in a storm, becoming foam that rises and completely fills the empty space of the can, while ice freezes it solid.
5. After the continued trot of two leagues, arrive at the place where deep dishes await to receive it. Remove the stoppers and two cascades of frozen foam fill the containers.
6. Season with sugar and cinnamon and serve to the table.
7. Alternatively, in the early morning hours, place a quantity of good milk on ice seasoned with saltpeter in a deep tin or zinc dish.
8. You may add one cup of cream and season it with sugar and cinnamon.
9. Make two bundles of straw used for brooms to use as spatulas.
10. Beat the milk from both sides of the dish with these two spatulas.
11. In ten minutes, the milk will have turned to foam and this foam will have reached complete freezing.

title: Helado de Fresas a la Crema (Strawberry Cream Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- cinnamon
- sugar
- very ripe strawberries
- essence of strawberries

## Directions

1. Have cold milk ready, then cook it with a light boil, seasoned with cinnamon and enough sugar to sweeten it well
2. Place very ripe strawberries in a dish and mash them with two spoons
3. Beat them further to finish breaking them down
4. Mix them with the milk and pass through a sieve
5. Add a few drops of strawberry essence to enhance the perfume
6. Place in an ice cream maker to freeze

title: Helado de Ponche (Punch Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Four dozen almonds, among which six bitter ones
- One coconut from Panama or Brazil, or lacking these, four dozen coconuts from Paraguay
- Milk
- Cinnamon
- Sugar, necessary amount to sweeten well
- One cup of natural cream
- Half a bottle of good cognac

## Directions

1. Grind four dozen almonds in the mortar, among which introduce six bitter ones.
2. Grind equally one coconut from Panama or Brazil, and lacking these, four dozen coconuts from Paraguay.
3. As they are being ground, successively add drops of milk to prevent them from becoming oily.
4. Once ground, pour hot milk over them separately, and strain them separately through a thin but strong cloth, pressing them so they yield all their juice.
5. Increase the juice by grinding them again and pouring hot milk over them anew, then strain them again.
6. Season with cinnamon and the necessary sugar to sweeten well.
7. Add one cup of natural cream and half a bottle of good cognac.
8. Stir and place to freeze, taking care that the freezing does not harden the liquid and remove its strength.

title: Helado de Sangria (Sangria Ice Cream)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- wine
- water
- ice
- sugar
- lemon
- cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare a sangria mixture with the traditional ingredients of wine, water, ice, sugar, lemon, cinnamon, and nutmeg
2. Freeze in an ice cream maker

title: Hostias al Ajonjolí (Sesame Seed Wafers)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of toasted and ground sesame seeds
- half a pound of toasted whole sesame seeds
- two pounds of ground almonds
- one pound of toasted and ground hazelnuts
- three ounces of powdered cinnamon
- one adarme of ground clove
- three pounds of bee honey
- three pounds of grated toasted bread
- half an ounce of grated nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a syrup from sugar, mix it with the bee honey, bring to an alfajorado point, add the previously mentioned ground ingredients, and allow to boil until it reaches a high point. Then remove from heat and pour over wafers, spreading a layer of the mixture on one wafer and covering with another, as done with a sandwich.

title: Huevo Colosal (Colossal Egg)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- two prepared bladders, one smaller than the other
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take two prepared bladders, inflated and dried, one smaller than the other
2. Break eggs, separating the whites from the yolks, until there is enough yolk to fill the small bladder
3. Fill the small bladder with yolks and, once well sealed in its round form, cook it in boiling water
4. When the yolk is hard, remove it from the water and, breaking the bladder, extract the yolk in the form it has taken, and place it inside the large bladder, which has previously been filled with whites
5. The yolk remains in the middle of the white
6. Close it also in its round form and place it in boiling water
7. When the white has acquired the necessary hardness in its cooking, break the bladder, from which comes out a colossal egg, which looks beautiful on the table, placed on a spicy sauce, with decorations of parsley and lettuce hearts, bathed in vinegar and oil, and seasoned with spices

title: Huevos a la Suprema (Supreme-Style Eggs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, eight
- lamb kidneys, two, cooked
- mushrooms, four, cooked
- asparagus, one dozen, cooked
- cheese, grated
- bread, cold and grated
- pure olive oil
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Break eight eggs and beat them partially
2. Cook two lamb kidneys, four mushrooms, and one dozen asparagus
3. Cut into small pieces the kidneys, mushrooms, and the asparagus tips
4. Mix with the eggs, add grated cheese and grated cold bread
5. Fry everything in pure olive oil and serve over tomato sauce

title: Huevos Fritos (Fried Eggs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- mantequilla (butter)
- una cabeza de cebolla (one onion head)
- un tomate (one tomato)
- un diente de ajo (one garlic clove)
- huevos (eggs)
- pimienta (pepper)
- sal (salt)
- pan (bread)

## Directions

1. Mince very finely one onion head, one tomato, and one garlic clove, and fry them in butter until the onion takes on a light golden color
2. Remove from the fire and pass through a sieve
3. Return to the pan and break the eggs over it, sprinkling pepper and salt over each one, and serve from the moment the whites have turned white
4. Serve with very thin, golden toasted bread slices arranged in a circle at the center of the plate, with one slice served per egg

title: Huevos Hilados (Fried Egg Strands)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks
- Butter

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks lightly and place them inside a fine cloth such as linen or clear gauze.
2. Squeeze them out in a thin stream into hot butter in a saucepan, held high above.
3. Two people should perform this operation: one strains the eggs and another uses two forks to spread them so they fry in separate strands.
4. Remove the fried strands with the forks as they cook and place on a napkin that absorbs the butter and leaves them golden and dry.
5. Use to garnish salads and lean dishes.

title: Huevos Rellenos (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen eggs
- a piece of meat from a stew pot
- roasted peanuts
- parsley
- white onion
- pepper
- salt
- a pinch of cumin
- muscatel raisins
- butter
- half a glass of sweet wine
- egg for coating
- breadcrumb shavings
- cheese shavings

## Directions

1. Boil a dozen eggs in water.
2. Remove a piece of cooked meat from a stew pot, cool it in water, dry it, and grind it in a mortar.
3. Grind the roasted peanuts.
4. Chop parsley and white onion.
5. Mix the ground meat with the peanuts, parsley, and onion, seasoning with pepper, salt, a pinch of cumin, and muscatel raisins.
6. Place a piece of freshly churned butter in a pan and heat until it begins to boil.
7. Add the prepared meat mixture to the butter, then add half a glass of sweet wine.
8. Cook for ten minutes and remove from heat.
9. Cool the mixture in a serving dish.
10. Peel the boiled eggs and cut them in half.
11. Remove the yolks and replace them with the meat filling.
12. Close the egg halves and wrap them in beaten egg thickened with breadcrumb and cheese shavings.
13. Fry them in butter that is already boiling in the pan.
14. Serve on a porcelain platter.

title: Huminta (Peruvian Fresh Corn Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- green corn cobs with grain full of sweet milk
- salt to taste
- a very little sugar
- good quantity of pork lard fried with ají
- corn husks
- water
- split cane sticks

## Directions

1. Grate the fresh corn, then grind it in a stone mortar.
2. Once well ground, season with salt to taste, a very little sugar, and a good quantity of pork lard fried with ají and previously strained.
3. Mix everything, stir and beat with a spoon.
4. On two corn husks placed in opposite directions for each huminta, place three spoonfuls of the paste in the center.
5. Fold and tie with twine.
6. To cook in a pot: fill one-third with water, cross split cane sticks over the water surface, arrange the humintas one on top of another, and boil for two hours.
7. Drain the water and serve the humintas in their wrappings.

title: Jamón (Ham)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- vinegar
- water
- four cloves of garlic, ground
- onion head
- mint
- parsley
- rosemary
- onion leaves
- string
- two bottles of red wine
- more rosemary
- mint
- bay leaves
- two cloves of garlic
- four cloves
- piece of cinnamon
- sugar, finely ground
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash the ham repeatedly and soak it submerged in water for twenty-four hours
2. Wash again and cook in a terra-cotta pot or enamel porcelain pot, well submerged in water saturated with vinegar, with four crushed cloves of garlic, an onion head, and a large bundle of mint, parsley, rosemary, and onion leaves tied with string
3. When the water reduces during boiling, add more water and continue boiling until the skin swells and is ready to come off
4. Remove from fire, take the ham out of the pot, wash and dry the pot for the second cooking
5. Remove the skin from the ham by hand, dry well with a cloth, and cook in the same pot in two bottles of red wine with another bundle of rosemary, mint, and bay leaves, two cloves of garlic, four cloves, and a piece of cinnamon
6. Cover and cook, turning occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom
7. Add water at least twice as it boils
8. When the meat feels tender to pressure, remove from fire, transfer to a platter, and let cool in a cool place, covered with a wire cover, before storing
9. If desired caramelized, once cooled, dust finely ground sugar mixed with a little cinnamon and pass a hot iron over it placed on paper

title: Jigote a la Panchita (Panchita's Jigote)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin slices of beef from the ham section
- Salt and pepper
- Vinegar
- Pure olive oil
- Broth
- Bread slices
- Sauce used for picadillo

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of beef from the ham section
2. Lightly grill them until they begin to release bloody juice
3. Half-grind them in a mortar
4. Chop them finely with a sharp knife
5. Put them in a pot with the picadillo sauce
6. Add grilled bread slices soaked in broth seasoned with pepper, vinegar, and pure olive oil
7. Cook over low heat for one hour
8. Remove and leave on the coals until time to serve
9. This dish is preferably used for dinner

title: Jigote de Pato a la Tartera (Duck Pâté in Terrine)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Piece of veal sirloin
- Fresh bacon
- Parsley
- Scallions
- Thyme
- Laurel
- One garlic clove
- Pepper
- One clove spice
- Salt
- Cognac
- Cooked parsley branches for garnish

## Directions

1. Debone the duck and mince it finely.
2. Mince together with a piece of veal sirloin, fresh bacon, parsley, scallions, thyme, laurel, one garlic clove, pepper, one clove spice, and salt to taste.
3. Grind everything very well and mix and incorporate into the minced meats.
4. Line a terrine with a well-fitting lid with strips of fresh bacon.
5. Over this layer of bacon, spread the minced meat mixture.
6. Add more pieces of bacon on top, sprinkle with one glass of cognac.
7. Cover with strips of bacon, and adjusting the lid carefully, place in the oven to cook for four hours.
8. Serve in the terrine, hidden in a serving dish with branches of parsley.

title: Leche Asada (Baked Milk)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- sugar to sweeten well
- 30 egg yolks
- a handful of wheat flour
- pieces of cinnamon
- live coals

## Directions

1. Fill a baking dish with milk and the necessary sugar to sweeten it well
2. Pass 30 egg yolks through a sieve and add to the milk
3. Add a handful of wheat flour and pieces of cinnamon
4. Put a pot of water on active fire
5. When the water is boiling, place the prepared milk dish on top of the pot as a lid
6. Stir with a spoon as if making mazamorra
7. When it comes to a boil, cover the dish with a metal sheet and place a layer of live coals on top while maintaining active fire under the pot of boiling water
8. When cooking is calculated to be done, uncover the dish and pierce the preparation with a straw; if it comes out dry, it is ready to serve

title: Leche Caramelo (Caramel Milk)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters milk
- 2.5 pounds sugar
- 4 ounces starch
- 18 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 6 ounces coconut, ground and moistened with milk
- 4 tablespoons hot milk
- 2 drops lemon essence
- powdered and sifted sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Cook the milk with sugar and starch, stirring constantly with a thin cane
2. When thickened, remove from fire and pour in the half-beaten egg yolks by the spoonful, adding a tablespoon of cinnamon
3. Return to fire, always stirring so it does not stick to the bottom
4. After a good boil, remove from fire
5. Prepare the coconut milk: grind the coconut very fine, moisten with milk, pass through strong cloth to extract all the juice, and mix with four tablespoons of hot milk
6. Pour the coconut preparation into the thickened milk off the fire, stirring vigorously to incorporate
7. Add two drops of lemon essence
8. Pour into a flat dish and let cool
9. When the surface no longer adheres to the touch of a finger, cover with a layer of powdered and sifted sugar
10. Pass a very hot metal sheet over the surface to caramelize it

title: Leche Frita (Fried Milk)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter very sweetened milk
- 4 to 6 orange leaves
- 4 ounces ground almonds, dissolved in milk
- sponge cake crumbs
- egg, beaten
- butter for frying
- sugar and cinnamon for coating

## Directions

1. Pour the egg yolks into the very sweetened milk and pass through a sieve
2. Put on fire and when it begins to boil, add four to six orange leaves
3. When thickened, mix in the ground almonds dissolved in milk and passed through a sieve
4. Let it reach a high point of thickening and pour into a dish or deep bowls over sponge cake crumbs
5. Let cool and cut with a knife tip into thin slices
6. Dip each slice in beaten egg and then in sponge cake crumbs
7. Fry in butter
8. Coat with sugar and cinnamon and serve

title: Leche Nevada (Snowy Milk)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter leche (unit) of milk
- One quarter leche of cream
- Sugar to taste
- Sponge cake slices (bizcochuelos)
- Natural cream for moistening
- Candied fruits for decoration

## Directions

1. Dissolve cream in a quarter portion of milk and sweeten to taste.
2. Beat with a hand mill (molinillo) until much foam forms.
3. Arrange a layer of sponge cake slices moistened with natural cream on a plate.
4. Spoon the foam by spoonfuls over the cake layer.
5. Serve decorated with candied fruits.

title: Lengua de Vaca a la Húngara (Cow Tongue Hungarian Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cow tongue
- water
- salt
- two heads of onion
- one clove of garlic
- parsley, oregano and spearmint
- knife
- thick needle
- two hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- half a head of onion, ground
- half a clove of garlic, ground
- raisins
- almonds, crushed
- butter
- vinegar
- olive oil
- bread and cheese, grated
- paper

## Directions

1. Wash the tongue well, scrape it with a knife, remove the hides, fats and membranes from the upper end, and prick it with a thick needle over its entire surface
2. Boil it for three hours in two liters of water with salt, two heads of onion, one clove of garlic and a small bunch of parsley, oregano and spearmint
3. Once cooked, strike it with the flat of a large knife so the skin loosens, and let it cool
4. Remove the skin and make a deep cut lengthwise in the thickest part
5. Through this cut, with a sharp knife, extract a piece of meat from the center
6. Mince this meat very finely with two hard-boiled eggs
7. Season with salt, pepper, half a head of ground onion and half a clove of ground garlic, raisins and crushed almonds
8. Put it on the fire with a little butter for six minutes, stirring with a spoon
9. Remove from fire and complete the seasoning with a little, very little vinegar and a drop of olive oil
10. Fill the hollow made in the tongue with this mixture
11. Close the edges of the filling with three turns of strong thread
12. Coat the tongue in butter, bread it in grated bread and cheese, and wrapped in a sheet of paper coated with butter, place it in the oven for a quarter hour
13. Serve with purée of fresh peas and pickled cornichons

title: Lomito Criollo (Creole Pork Tenderloin)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- chili pepper
- garlic
- cumin
- pepper
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- bitter orange juice
- onions
- pork fat
- sweet potato

## Directions

1. Choose the fattest part of the pork loin and cut it into pieces the size and shape of steaks.
2. Immerse each piece in a marinade composed of powdered chili pepper, garlic, cumin, pepper, and salt, with a little oil and vinegar.
3. Remove to a platter and cover for two hours to marinate well.
4. Separately, make the sauce: in bitter orange juice, place thinly sliced onions, well-ground chili pepper, and salt, and let it pickle for four hours.
5. Fry the pork loins in very hot pork fat until they brown.
6. Place them on a platter and pour the sauce with the orange juice over them.
7. Arrange boiled sweet potato pieces as garnish.

title: Macarrones a la Calabresa (Calabrese-Style Macaroni)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Cheese, grated
- Bread crumbs, finely crumbled
- Cheese, grated
- Butter, melted

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni.
2. Spread grated cheese on the bottom of a heat-resistant platter.
3. Place the cooked macaroni over the cheese.
4. Even the surface and scatter over the macaroni a mixture of finely crumbled bread crumbs and grated cheese in equal parts.
5. Pour melted butter over this mixture.
6. Place in the oven with fire on top of the lid.
7. It is ready when the cheese on the surface has taken on a good color.
8. Serve in the same platter in which it was made.

title: Manjar Real (Royal Delicacy)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of sugar
- a pound and a half of ground almonds
- half a bottle of milk
- three ounces of wheat starch

## Directions

1. Make a high-point syrup with four pounds of sugar
2. Let it cool slightly and add a pound and a half of ground almonds
3. Meanwhile, dissolve and cook three ounces of wheat starch in half a bottle of milk
4. When thick and cooked, mix with the almond syrup
5. Return to the heat and allow to reach the proper point

title: Manjarcina de Mojarra a la Pschut (Delicate Mojarra Preparation)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- mojarras (small fish)
- salt
- water
- butter
- pure olive oil
- grape vinegar
- broth or stock
- mushrooms
- onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Open, clean, and scale the mojarras well, then wash with well-salted water
2. Finish opening them with three strikes on the back with the handle of a mortar
3. Cut off the tail and head
4. Arrange them in a pan with skin side down with a proportionate amount of melted butter and four tablespoons of pure olive oil
5. Place the pan over high heat, stirring the mojarras once or twice so they cook evenly
6. Once cooked, remove them from the pan
7. In the same pan (which should be earthenware or iron enameled with porcelain), pour half a glass of grape vinegar, a cup of broth or good stock, a dozen mushrooms cut into pieces, onion slices, a tablespoon of finely chopped parsley, salt and pepper
8. Reduce this sauce over high heat
9. When serving, place the mojarras in a serving dish, add half a cup of melted butter to the sauce, and pour it boiling over the fish

title: Marrasquino de Leche (Milk Maraschino Liqueur)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1500 grams alcohol
- 1000 grams distilled water
- 500 grams sugar
- 2 grams mace
- 4 grams cloves
- 1.50 grams grated lemon zest
- 0.50 centigrams anise
- 30 bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Mix all these ingredients together
2. Place in infusion in a crystal or porcelain vessel for eight days
3. Filter

title: Masa Imperial (Imperial Dough)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of wheat flour
- half a pound of butter
- eight eggs, separated
- a small glass of cognac
- cinnamon
- sugar to taste
- plum preserve

## Directions

1. Mix and beat very well one pound of wheat flour and half a pound of butter
2. Beat the egg yolks and whites separately
3. When they reach meringue point, mix yolks and whites together
4. Add them to the flour mixture and beat very well with a small glass of cognac, cinnamon, and sugar to taste
5. Prepare a plum preserve and spread a layer of it on the bottom of a heat-resistant porcelain dish
6. Pour the dough on top, spread it in the shape of a cake, and place in a very hot oven, as cooking should be rapid
7. Serve in the same dish, without relief decorations

title: Mazamorra de Leche (Milk Cornmeal Porridge)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- cinnamon
- sugar
- anise
- flour
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Season the milk with cinnamon, sugar and anise
2. Put on fire, and after two boils, remove, pass through a sieve, and add the flour that has been dissolved in milk
3. Return to fire and stir constantly until the flour has cooked and thickened
4. Remove again from fire, and add, without ceasing to stir, 3 well-beaten egg yolks
5. Leave on fire for one minute and serve on plates, sprinkling with sugar and cinnamon
6. Serve cold

title: Mazamorra Morada (Purple Corn Dessert)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- regular amount of water
- purple corn
- corn cobs
- 6 to 8 pieces of pineapple with skin
- 2 dozen dried cherries
- pineapple, peeled and in slices
- dried cherries
- dried peaches
- quince strips
- sugar
- sweet potato starch
- 1 tablespoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a regular amount of water, add the shelled purple corn, cooking also in the same water with its cobs, as well as 6 to 8 pieces of pineapple with skin and 2 dozen dried cherries
2. Cook 4 hours so that the corn and cobs release all their juice and color, giving the liquid a beautiful purple color
3. Pass through a sieve, and one part of that liquid is put to boil in an enameled pan with sliced peeled pineapple, dried cherries, dried peaches cut into pieces and pitted, strips of quince, and sufficient sugar to sweeten well
4. Boil until cooked well
5. Dissolve sweet potato starch in the purple water and pour little by little, spoonful by spoonful, into the pan in which the cooked is boiling, and when adding the starch, stir constantly so that no clots form
6. Let cook, stirring always, until the mazamorra, cooked, reaches the point of dulce de leche
7. Then, a moment before removing from fire, mix in 1 tablespoon of lemon juice

title: Mentiritas de Cordero (Little Lamb Lies)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- young lamb ribs
- cooked potato
- three eggs
- parsley
- celery
- pepper
- salt
- pork fat from fresh bacon
- olives
- pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Peel and scrape the lamb ribs well with a knife.
2. Grind the meat and mix with an equal quantity of cooked and ground potato.
3. Add three eggs, parsley, celery, pepper, and salt.
4. Incorporate everything well by stirring with a spoon.
5. Divide into portions according to the number of cleaned ribs.
6. Place the cleaned bone in the middle of each portion, shaping them like ribs.
7. Roll them in beaten egg, then in breadcrumbs.
8. Fry in pork fat extracted from fresh bacon.
9. Serve without sauce, with garnishes of olives and pickled cucumbers.

title: Migas (Bread Crumb Dish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of pork fat
- two large onion heads, finely minced
- four cloves of garlic, ground
- one tablespoon of paprika
- salt to taste
- five medium breads soaked in milk
- half a pound of fresh cheese, minced
- six egg yolks, lightly beaten
- olives
- pieces of boiled yuca

## Directions

1. Heat half a pound of pork fat and fry the finely minced onions, ground garlic, paprika, and salt to taste
2. When the onion has taken on color, add five medium breads soaked in milk, lightly squeezed, and broken up, stirring continuously to prevent burning
3. When it comes to a boil, add half a pound of minced fresh cheese
4. When everything is well incorporated and a moment before removing from the heat, add six lightly beaten egg yolks
5. Serve with garnishes of olives and pieces of boiled yuca

title: Mixtura a la Huri (Huri's Mixture)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of toasted and ground sesame seeds
- one pound of ground almonds
- three pounds of sugar
- one ounce of powdered cinnamon
- half an adarme of ground clove
- a pinch of ginger
- two ounces of fresh butter
- one glass of virgin or bee honey
- a pinch of ground nutmeg
- three pounds of toasted bread, finely grated

## Directions

1. Make a high-point syrup with three pounds of sugar mixed with the honey. Remove from heat to incorporate the grated bread and butter. Return to heat and stir until perfectly mixed. Remove from heat again to mix in the cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, and ginger, allowing it to rest for one hour. Then add the sesame seeds and almonds. Place on heat and bring to the desired consistency according to taste.

title: Mondongo (Lamb Tripe Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- the thickest part of lamb belly
- hot water
- one or two onion heads, depending on quantity
- pork fat
- a clove of garlic, ground
- juice of a tomato passed through a sieve
- pepper
- cumin
- four tablespoons of broth
- bread crumb softened in milk
- two egg yolks, beaten
- vinegar and oil
- a glass of heavy cream or good milk

## Directions

1. Take the thickest part of the lamb belly and wash it very well several times with hot water, scraping it each time with a knife
2. Wash until all odor is lost
3. Give it three boils, changing the water and washing it again in each change
4. Cook until it falls apart under pressure
5. Chop into small pieces and place in a sauce prepared as follows:
6. Chop very finely one or two onion heads depending on quantity and fry in a pan with pork fat, a clove of ground garlic, the juice of a tomato passed through a sieve, a little pepper and a little, very little cumin
7. When the onion is lightly golden, add four tablespoons of broth and let it boil a little more
8. Mix in a proportionate quantity of bread crumb softened in milk, to which two beaten egg yolks and a little vinegar and oil have been previously mixed
9. Incorporate the chopped tripe to the sauce and give it half a boil
10. When serving, still in the pan, mix in a glass of heavy cream or good milk

title: Mondonguito a la Sultana (Tripe Sultan-Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tripe, cleaned
- White onion, chopped
- Garlic, ground
- Oregano
- Pepper powder
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Lettuce leaves
- Lettuce hearts
- Olive oil and vinegar for dressing

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb tripe thoroughly several times, scrape it, and cook it.
2. Chop it very fine and place it in a platter to marinate with a mixture of chopped white onion, ground garlic, oregano and pepper powder, oil and vinegar.
3. Let it marinate for two hours.
4. Serve in a platter on lettuce leaves, with lettuce hearts dressed in oil and vinegar on top.

title: Morcilla o Budin Negro (Black Pudding)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- liquid pork blood
- salt
- biscuit or crackers with water
- three dozen almonds, peeled
- three dozen walnuts
- a handful of roasted peanuts
- one head of garlic
- oregano
- pepper
- green onion, finely chopped
- one cup of raw cream
- three eggs, beaten
- fresh bacon, finely chopped
- sausage casings of regular thickness
- wire loop
- string

## Directions

1. Take liquid pork blood and mix with a little salt to prevent coagulation
2. Grate biscuit or crackers with water in proportion to the quantity of blood
3. Peel and grind three dozen almonds, three dozen walnuts, and a handful of roasted peanuts, along with one head of garlic, oregano, and pepper
4. Pulverize everything in a mortar and mix with the blood
5. Add finely chopped green onion, one cup of raw cream, three beaten eggs, and plenty of fresh bacon, finely chopped
6. Mix everything thoroughly, stirring well in all directions
7. Using a small wire loop at the opening of the casing and a spoon, stuff the filling into casings of regular thickness
8. Tie the ends and separations with string
9. Boil, taking care to prick the casing in various places with a pin to prevent it from bursting from the heat
10. Hang with the same precautions as the sausage

title: Natitas a la Czarina (Czarina's Natitas)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of sponge cake
- heavy cream
- butter
- cinnamon
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Soak slices of sponge cake in heavy cream and arrange on a dish previously buttered, alternating with layers of whipped cream dusted with cinnamon and powdered sugar. The bottom layer should be sponge cake and the top layer should be cream. With a thick dusting of sugar, place in a well-heated oven for fifteen minutes.

title: Nogada de Bacalao (Walnut Sauce with Cod)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- vinegar
- water
- garlic
- green onion leaves
- walnuts
- milk
- onion heads
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- oil
- broth
- pickled cucumbers
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Soak the cod for ten hours in cold water saturated with vinegar, stirring three times
2. Wash it again with three waters, then cook for one hour in water saturated with vinegar with one garlic clove and green onion leaves unpicked
3. Remove from heat, wash again with two waters, cut into small pieces, remove bones and spines, and place in a dish to infuse in water with vinegar
4. Break walnuts and drop them in boiling water briefly to loosen the skin, remove the skin by washing in cold water until white, then grind well
5. Mix the ground walnuts with boiling milk, passing them through cheesecloth or linen cloth and pressing well so the thick walnut juice comes out with the milk
6. Make a sauce with cooked onion heads separated leaf by leaf (unpicked), one ground garlic clove, pepper, a little breadcrumbs, salt to taste, and butter
7. Mix, bring to a boil briefly, add five tablespoons of vinegar and three of oil, mix in the cod, add three tablespoons of broth, and boil for fifteen minutes, stirring gently
8. Remove from heat, mix in the walnut milk, and serve garnished with pickled cucumbers and hard-boiled egg slices

title: Nogada de Pollo (Walnut Sauce with Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- walnuts
- milk
- onion heads
- garlic
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- broth
- pickled cucumbers
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Roast a good chicken on a grill with bright heat, turning it carefully so it does not burn
2. Bone it and prepare it in the same sauce and walnut milk as the cod
3. Follow the same preparation method as Nogada de Bacalao, using the prepared chicken instead of cod
4. Serve with the same garnishes

title: Olla o Puchero Limeño (Peruvian Beef and Pork Stew)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef from the chest
- four legs of white pork, cleaned of hair and scraped
- a piece of salted beef tongue, previously washed and soaked
- a piece of bacon, fresh or salted
- a bouquet of aromatic herbs composed of parsley, mint or lemon balm, basil, rosemary and bay leaf, tied with string around an onion head with green leaves
- a cabbage, well washed and tied in four turns with twine
- a clove of toasted garlic
- peppercorns
- cabbage leaves for the surface
- salt
- two handfuls of chickpeas
- two toasted garlic cloves
- a tomate
- parsley
- meat fat
- paprika
- four cabbage leaves that have cooked on the surface of the pot
- half a teaspoon of pepper
- half a teaspoon of salt
- half a teaspoon of cumin
- three tablespoons of vinegar and oil
- a little broth from the pot
- potatoes
- sweet potatoes
- fruits for garnish

## Directions

1. Choose a piece of beef from the chest part
2. Take four legs of white pork, well cleaned of hair, scraped with a knife, and washed, as well as the meat, in lukewarm water lightly saturated with vinegar
3. Place together with a piece of salted beef tongue, previously washed and soaked in hot water, in a pot with a good quantity of water over a brisk fire
4. Skim carefully, then add a piece of bacon, fresh or salted, and a bouquet of aromatic herbs composed of parsley, mint or lemon balm, basil, rosemary and bay leaf, tied with string around an onion head with green leaves
5. Add a well-washed cabbage tied in four turns with twine to prevent it from falling apart during boiling, a clove of toasted garlic and peppercorns
6. Cover the surface of the pot with a layer of cabbage leaves; being careful to add very little salt because of the salted tongue, cover the pot and let it boil for three hours over a brisk fire
7. Remove from the fire to extract half the broth and place a sausage and an unsalted stuffing or morcilla
8. Prepare the seasoning by grinding separately in a mortar two handfuls of chickpeas with a clove of toasted garlic, a tomato and parsley, meat fat, paprika and four cabbage leaves that have cooked on the surface of the pot
9. When all is well ground, mix; add half a teaspoon of pepper, half a teaspoon each of salt and cumin, three tablespoons each of vinegar and oil, with a little of the broth extracted from the pot
10. Beat lightly and pour over the stew, covering again with cabbage leaves, to cook again over low heat, well covered with a tight lid
11. On the new layer of cabbage leaves, place potatoes, sweet potatoes, and fruits to be cooked by steam, which will serve as garnish for the lima stew

title: Ostras a la Judic (Oysters Judic Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Three dozen oysters
- Butter, one piece
- Fine herbs
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Pure olive oil, one tablespoon
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bread crumbs, hard and grated
- Lemon, one, juiced

## Directions

1. Open three dozen oysters; collect the water they contain, strain it through cloth, and once heated and about to boil, place the oysters in it for four minutes.
2. Remove them from the water immediately, drain them, and place them in a dish that can resist the action of fire, with a piece of butter and a seasoning of fine herbs and chopped mushrooms, one tablespoon of pure olive oil, and a strong seasoning of pepper and nutmeg.
3. Cover all with a layer of hard bread crumbs, grated, and keep the dish over gentle fire and covered with a portable stove provided with lively heat.
4. When the bread crumbs have taken on a golden color, remove the dish from the heat and serve, sprinkling the oysters with the juice of one lemon.

title: Pachamanca (Earthen Pit-Roasted Kid Goat)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- kid goat at weaning age
- oil
- vinegar
- mirasol chili pepper
- cumin
- fresh cheese
- aged and buttery cheese from Tafí or Huaraz
- potatoes
- sweet potato
- yucca or cassava
- corn cobs
- plantain leaves
- wood
- stones
- earth
- strong wool or linen cloth

## Directions

1. Skin the kid goat, wash it, and cut off the head and legs.
2. Place it in a marinade of oil, vinegar, and spices (especially mirasol chili pepper and cumin) in a wooden or non-metal vessel for two hours, stirring every half hour to marinate evenly.
3. Prepare thick slices of fresh cheese and aged, buttery cheese from Tafí or Huaraz; potatoes, sweet potato, yucca or cassava, and corn cobs, all whole with their skins.
4. Have ready the largest plantain leaves.
5. Dig a half-meter-deep pit in a dry location in the open air, preferably in sandy soil, with enough width so that the items to be roasted do not hinder the meat, which should be laid out extended, and potatoes, yucca, corn cobs, and cheese piled to one side.
6. Place a large quantity of wood in the pit and cover it with stones, forming an oven and raising it to a height of one meter.
7. Before setting fire to the wood, arrange the plantain leaves on top of the stones in pairs, wrapped to soak and lose their rigidity and become flexible enough to wrap the pieces to be roasted.
8. Light the wood; the flame rises and burns all the wood, which becomes live coals and hot ash, falling to the bottom of the pit and heating the stones.
9. After the initial flames, remove the roasted plantain leaves, unfold them, and wrap in them the kid goat and cheese.
10. With a shovel, move the stones to the edges of the oven.
11. Arrange the kid goat and cheese in the bottom, cleared of coals and embers, and add piled potatoes, yucca, sweet potato, and corn cobs.
12. Pour the coals and embers over them again.
13. Add the extracted earth from the pit and even the stones, and cover everything with a strong cloth of wool or linen.
14. After five hours of cooking, the pachamanca is ready.
15. Carefully remove the cloth, stones, earth, coals, and embers.
16. Carefully extract the roasted pieces and free them from their plantain leaf wrapping.
17. Serve on large platters with spicy sauces.

title: Panquitas a la Picantera (Spicy Corn Cakes)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh corn cobs, grated and ground
- pork lard
- salt
- ground red ají, to desired degree
- green onion, finely chopped
- ground cumin
- corn husks or panc
- water
- split cane sticks

## Directions

1. Grate and grind the fresh corn well.
2. Knead with plenty of pork lard, salt, and ground red ají with water to the desired degree.
3. Cook in pork lard finely chopped green onion, sprinkle a little ground cumin over it, and mix with the corn, beating it very well so that all ingredients incorporate.
4. Wrap in corn husks, placing two spoonfuls for each husk.
5. Fold and tie with twine.
6. Bake in the oven, or cook in a pot with little water, arranged in piles on cane sticks crossed over the boiling water surface, being careful to cover the pot mouth to conserve the necessary steam for cooking.

title: Papilla (Pap)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of sweet potatoes
- a quarter portion of milk
- high-point syrup

## Directions

1. Cook four pounds of sweet potatoes
2. Peel them and dissolve in a quarter portion of milk
3. Strain through a sieve and mix with high-point syrup

title: Papilla (Sweet Potato Paste)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of sweet potatoes
- 1/4 of milk
- 5 pounds of sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon of cinnamon
- 2 cups of Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Cook 4 pounds of sweet potatoes
2. Peel them and dissolve in 1/4 of milk, strain through a sieve
3. Mix with syrup made in high point with 5 pounds of sugar
4. Stir and cook over fire until it reaches the point of dulce de leche
5. Remove from fire and add 12 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon of cinnamon, and 2 cups of Sherry wine
6. Beat together and pour spoon by spoon over the papilla, stirring constantly to prevent the egg from breaking when cooking
7. Return to fire, stirring always, and when it begins to boil, remove from fire and pour into dessert dishes
8. Beat much so that it whitens

title: Pasta de Nuez (Walnut Paste)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound peeled walnuts, well ground
- Breadcrumbs from a criolla bread
- Twelve egg yolks
- Half pound pulverized sugar
- Sponge cake (bizcochuelo) slices
- One cup sweetened milk sprinkled with cinnamon
- Four tablespoons ground walnuts
- Three egg whites
- Half pound pulverized sugar for the glaze

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of peeled, ground walnuts with breadcrumbs from a criolla bread, twelve egg yolks, and half pound of pulverized sugar.
2. Stir and beat this mixture well.
3. Make a walnut sauce (nogada) by combining one cup of sweetened milk sprinkled with cinnamon with four tablespoons of ground walnuts, straining the mixture through a sieve.
4. Place sponge cake slices soaked in the walnut sauce as a base in a dish.
5. Arrange the walnut paste on top of this base.
6. Bake in a hot oven for a quarter hour.
7. Remove and coat with a glaze (nevado) made from three beaten egg whites and half pound of pulverized sugar beaten to meringue consistency.
8. Return to a warm oven to set the glaze.

title: Pastel a la Cambray (Cambrai Pastry Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry with powdered sugar mixed into the layers with flour and butter
- 6 bananas, sliced thin
- 1/2 medium pineapple, sliced thin
- 6 peaches cut into slices with pits
- 12 Tours plums
- 6 cherries, fresh and raw
- juice of 3 oranges
- 24 grape grains
- 2 pounds ground sugar
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 1 glass good Priorat wine

## Directions

1. Prepare a puff pastry with powdered sugar mixed into its layers
2. Cut bananas and pineapple into thin slices
3. Blanch the peaches cut into slices with their pits and the Tours plums with two boilings
4. Mix all the fruits, both blanched and raw, with the orange juice
5. Add the ground sugar, cinnamon, and a glass of good Priorat wine
6. Cover well and let marinate for two hours
7. Arrange this mixture in a porcelain baking dish resistant to fire
8. Cover with the puff pastry
9. Bake in a moderate oven so the pastry cooks delicately
10. Decorate as desired

title: Pastel de Choclo a la Sucrense (Corn Cake Sucre Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- corn (choclo)
- milk
- white cornmeal or cornstarch
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Grate the corn and grind it very well in a mortar or mill. Pour in one cup of milk, mix well, and strain through loose cloth, squeezing hard to extract the juice.
2. Return the pulp to the mortar and grind again, also adding one cup of milk, and strain.
3. Into this corn juice, add white cornmeal or cornstarch spoon by spoon, stirring as you add the flour and beating until it has thickened.
4. Season with salt and a little sugar, at most one or two spoonfuls, to bring out the natural sweetness of the corn.
5. Melt a large piece of butter and mix it into the mixture, stirring and beating until the butter is incorporated.
6. If the mixture has thickened too much, add a little milk and, always stirring, place it to cook over moderate heat.
7. Taste frequently to know by flavor when it is cooked and ready.
8. Remove from heat, add butter, stir and let cool. Once cold, mix in four egg yolks and stir to incorporate them into the mixture.
9. Butter the bottom of a heat-resistant earthenware dish and spread a layer of the corn mixture. Over this arrange a filling of your choice, either squab prepared in sauce and stewed, or a chopped mixture seasoned with spices, Malaga raisins, almonds and olives.
10. On the filling, arrange symmetrically slices of hard-boiled eggs and olives. Cover the filling with another layer of corn mixture and put in the oven.
11. When the surface of the cake has taken a deep golden color, cooking is done, and it should go from the oven to the table; the hotter this cake is, the more exquisite it becomes.

title: Pastel de Frejoles (Bean Pie)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beans (porotos)
- butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup milk
- fresh bacon
- 2 tablespoons pure olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- 1 clove garlic
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Soak 1 pound of beans in cold water for ten hours.
2. Carefully remove the skin covering them; wash them, give them a boil, and grind them in a mortar.
3. Place them in a plate or deep dish with a piece of butter, and stir and beat with 1 tablespoon of flour and 1 cup of milk.
4. Fry fresh bacon in small pieces, which is mixed into the dough. Give it a final beating by adding 2 tablespoons of pure olive oil, and season with salt, pepper, and 1 clove of ground garlic.
5. Prepare a mold lined with butter, making sure the bottom has a thick layer of butter; pour the dough on top, over it a very thin puff pastry, and put it in the oven until the pastry has browned.

title: Pastel de Pescado (Fish Pastry)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- red wine vinegar
- onion slices
- garlic clove
- pepper
- lard
- breadcrumbs
- nutmeg
- hard-boiled eggs
- broth
- puff pastry
- butter
- green olives
- shrimp tails

## Directions

1. Place the fish for eight hours in a marinade of red wine vinegar, onion slices, one ground garlic clove and whole pepper. This is understood after it has been washed and the bones removed.
2. Then chop very finely, fry in lard, mix in breadcrumbs, a little nutmeg, chopped hard-boiled eggs and broth in very small quantity so it does not become watery.
3. Arrange all this in a dish that can resist the heat of cooking; covering the filling with puff pastry, put in the oven, taking care to place on the pastry a paper spread with butter to prevent it from burning, removing it five minutes before taking the pastry from the oven.
4. Serve with garnishes of green olives and shrimp tails.

title: Pastel de Pichones (Squab Pastry)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- cold water
- salt
- sugar
- suet
- egg yolks
- squabs
- butter
- white onion
- pepper
- cumin
- Malaga raisins
- vinegar
- hard-boiled eggs
- olives

## Directions

1. Take one pound of flour and knead it by adding little by little cold water lightly saturated with salt and sugar.
2. Make a stiff dough, which will be softened by kneading it with both hands on a table. Once softened a bit, mix in it a small quantity of suet and the yolks of two eggs.
3. Knead again with the suet and egg, then extend with a rolling pin until it is thin.
4. Then spread over that entire flattened surface, by means of a feather or with the hand, melted suet; sprinkle flour over that suet, fold in two and pass the rolling pin again, extending and thinning the dough, and again spread suet and sprinkle flour, up to six times.
5. Then turn the dish or vessel in which it is to be arranged over the dough; take the measure and cut around the dough with the tip of a knife.
6. Filling the dish, arrange the squab filling which will be prepared as follows: Wash the squabs and their offal, give them a boil, and after letting them cool, cut them apart; setting aside the offal, put the pieces and give them another boil in a sauce of butter, chopped white onion, pepper, a little cumin, Malaga raisins, half a tablespoon of vinegar, sufficient salt and the offal, which will be arranged between piece and piece, as well as olives and hard-boiled eggs in slices.
7. With the filling arranged and the edge of the dish buttered, cover with the puff pastry and put in the oven, after spreading the surface of it very lightly with butter and sprinkling sugar over it.

title: Pastelitos de Ayuno (Fasting Little Pastries)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dozen almonds
- almond milk
- flour
- pure olive oil
- chard leaves
- salt
- water
- potatoes
- green peas
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel 6 dozen almonds in boiling water and after washing them, grind them in a mortar.
2. Place them in a cup, reduced to paste, and by adding warm water strain them through clear cloth, squeezing well to extract the juice.
3. Grind the pulp still further, and strain and squeeze again.
4. Mix the almond milk that results from this operation with flour, kneading it, adding little by little as you knead, 3 or 4 tablespoons of pure, fresh olive oil; and when the kneaded dough, with the help of the oil, is soft and flexible, stretch it with a rolling pin, fold it six times, making it into a puff pastry, which thinned with the rolling pin, is cut into small squares, and filled with the following preparation:
5. Take chard leaves, wash them and give them a boil in salted water, taking care to cover the vessel to prevent the chard from turning black on contact with air.
6. Once cooked, remove from heat; remove from the leaves stems and fibers, and well cleaned of all this, chop finely.
7. Cook potatoes unpeeled; and once cooked peel them and chop very well, and mix with the chard, adding very well-cooked green peas. Season with pepper, salt, oil and vinegar.
8. With everything well incorporated, fill the little pastries, which are put in a very hot oven, because their cooking should be brief: just the time for the pastries to brown: ten minutes.

title: Pastelitos de Huevo a la Nena (Egg Little Pastries à la Nena)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- butter
- eggs
- salt
