- pepper
- thick cream of raw milk
- lard

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry dough, and well stretched with a rolling pin, cut it into 8-centimeter squares, which are laid out on a tablecloth.
2. In a pan placed over a brisk fire, put a piece of butter.
3. When the butter, already melted, is very hot, break eggs over it, sprinkling them with salt and pepper.
4. From the moment the white turns opaque, take the egg carefully with two spoons, and place it on the square of puff pastry, covering it with another, after having poured over the egg 1 tablespoon of thick cream of raw milk.
5. The lower and upper edges of the little pastry are sealed by moistening them inside with a finger dipped in milk.
6. They are fried in lard for lunch. But, to serve them at dinner, it is better to cook them in the oven.

title: Pato a la Limeña (Duck Lima Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Duck giblets (liver, gizzard, heart)
- Stock
- Butter
- Four eggs
- Grated bread
- Milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- Almonds
- Málaga raisins
- Olives
- Roasted tomato juice
- Parsley
- Peanuts
- Cream or good milk
- Four cooked egg yolks
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Debone the duck carefully, keeping it whole.
2. Cook the giblets (liver, gizzard, heart) in the cooking stock.
3. Chop the cooked giblets very finely, along with a piece of cooked meat.
4. Melt and warm a piece of butter, beat it until foamy.
5. Mix in four eggs, grated bread, milk, salt, pepper, almonds, and Málaga raisins.
6. Combine this mixture with the chopped meats, add olives, and fill the duck with this stuffing.
7. Sew the duck closed with a few stitches to prevent the filling from spilling out.
8. Place in the oven.
9. Prepare a sauce: either made from roasted tomato juice strained through a sieve, with ground parsley and ground peanuts from the mortar, seasoned with oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper; or made from parsley, grated toasted bread, cream or good milk, four cooked and ground egg yolks, and the necessary seasoning of oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
10. Arrange the cooked duck in its serving dish and pour the sauce over it.
11. Remove the stitches holding the filling in place.

title: Pavo Embozado a la Regente (Turkey Wrapped Regent-Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey
- Two dozen beaten eggs
- Three loaves of fat, grated
- Half a pound of cheese, grated
- Turkey blood and giblets
- One ounce of ground parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four tablespoons of olive oil
- Matambre (meat roll)
- Onions
- Garlic
- Vinegar
- Olive oil
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Butter
- Tomato juice
- Fine herbs
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Toasted bread slices
- Consomé

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey in the customary manner
2. Stuff it with a mixture of two dozen beaten eggs, three grated loaves of fat, half a pound of grated cheese, the cooked and chopped blood and giblets of the turkey, one ounce of ground parsley, salt, pepper and four tablespoons of olive oil, mixing everything when filling
3. Wrap it in a matambre that has been marinated for eight hours in a marinade of ground onions and garlic, vinegar, olive oil, pepper, salt, oregano and cumin
4. With strong thread and a thick needle, sew the edges of the matambre from top to bottom over the turkey's loin, covering it entirely
5. Coat the matambre with butter and put it in a hot oven
6. When the matambre has taken on a dark golden color, bring it to the oven door to turn the turkey to the other side and put it back in
7. When this side is equally browned, remove it from the oven, remove the matambre, coat it with butter, and put it back in the oven to brown for a moment and finish cooking
8. Serve it in slices over a sauce of tomato juice, chopped fine herbs, oil, vinegar, pepper and pieces of bread toasted in boiling consomé

title: Pejerreyes a la Comodoro (Comodoro-Style Silverside)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion heads
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Tomatoes, seeded
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Butter
- Pepper
- Cumin
- Pejerreyes (silverside fish)
- Water
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pickled onions
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Mince very finely onion heads, hard-boiled eggs and seeded tomatoes; coat with bread crumbs, season with salt, and fry in butter.
2. Once fried, dust with pepper and cumin, and use this to stuff the silverside that will have been cleaned, scaled, and with their spines removed.
3. Arrange them in a fish cooking vessel with water and salt, and covered with its lid, give them one hour of boiling over good heat.
4. Remove them carefully from the fish vessel and transfer them to the one that is to take them to the table.
5. Add oil, vinegar and pepper, and serve with garnishes of pickled onions and sprigs of parsley.

title: Pepián de Choclo (Corn Pepian)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- corn (choclo), grated and well ground
- broth, one cup
- onion, abundant
- tomatoes
- garlic, a little
- pepper, a little
- yellow chili, well ground, much
- salt
- poached chicken pieces or pork meat
- white corn flour, one or two tablespoons if needed

## Directions

1. Grate the corn and grind it very well in a mortar
2. Add one cup of broth and strain through a thin cloth, squeezing very well to extract the juice
3. Make a sauce with abundant onion, tomatoes, a little garlic and a little pepper, much well ground yellow chili and salt
4. Once cooked, add the corn and pieces of poached chicken or pork meat
5. Boil everything together until it thickens
6. If it has not thickened enough, add one or two tablespoons of white corn flour, diluting it in broth, stirring constantly so it does not stick
7. When you know by the taste that it is cooked, remove from fire and send to the table

title: Perdices a la Borrachita (Partridges Drunken Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Onions
- Two tomatoes
- Two tablespoons oil
- Two tablespoons red vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Half a glass of spirits
- Fresh bacon strips

## Directions

1. In a glazed earthenware or iron cooking vessel, place onions cut into quarters, two deseeded tomatoes, two tablespoons of oil, and two tablespoons of red vinegar.
2. Add salt, pepper, and half a glass of spirits.
3. Arrange the partridges on top, surrounded above and below with strips of fresh bacon.
4. Cover tightly so that steam does not escape.
5. Do not stir, but shake frequently to prevent burning on the bottom.

title: Perdices Arrepolladas (Partridges with Cabbage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Onions
- Two carrots
- Celery
- Parsley
- Fresh bacon
- Well-made sausage
- Cabbage
- One liter of stock
- Nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the partridges.
2. Place them in a cooking vessel with onions, two carrots, celery, parsley, fresh bacon, a well-made sausage, and a good cabbage that has been soaked in boiling water and well wrung out.
3. Pour one liter of stock seasoned with nutmeg over everything.
4. Let simmer for three hours.
5. To serve, place the cabbage at the bottom of the serving dish, arrange the partridges on top, alternating with pieces of sausage and bacon cut into small pieces.

title: Pescado Frito a la Limeña (Lima-Style Fried Fish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Corbina or other fish, freshly caught
- Garlic
- Onion
- Pepper
- Oregano
- Salt
- Vinegar, one tablespoon
- Yellow corn flour
- Lard

## Directions

1. Descale the fish and wash it with three waters, coat it internally and externally with a compound of garlic, onion, pepper, oregano and salt, all ground, and moistened with one tablespoon of vinegar.
2. Leave it in this marinade for two hours.
3. Then cut it into crosswise pieces; coat it in yellow corn flour and fry it in lard; first over not very lively fire to allow the fish to cook; then raising the heat so that it browns.

title: Picadillo (Ground Meat Hash)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the ham section
- Two or three onion heads
- A bunch of parsley and spearmint
- Juice of one roasted tomato passed through a sieve
- Two or three ounces of pork fat from fresh bacon, fried
- Broth
- Vinegar
- Some almonds
- Raisins from Málaga
- A pinch of cumin
- Egg slices for garnish

## Directions

1. Wash the beef and cook it with two boils
2. Remove from heat, dry with a napkin, and grind in a mortar or with a stone
3. Shred it and chop it finely with a knife
4. Grind and shred again
5. Chop the onions and parsley with spearmint according to quantity
6. Add the tomato juice and put everything in an enameled iron or porcelain pot with the pork fat
7. Cook until the onion turns golden
8. Place the ground meat in this sauce and add a few tablespoons of broth, a little vinegar, some almonds and Málaga raisins, and a pinch of cumin
9. Cook over moderate heat
10. After half an hour of cooking, remove from heat but keep warm
11. If it has dried too much, add two tablespoons of broth
12. Serve with egg slices as garnish

title: Pichones a la Delicieux (Squab Delicious-Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Squab giblets
- Beaten eggs
- Toasted bread
- Almonds
- Raisins from Málaga
- Pepper
- Salt
- Cumin
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pluck the squabs very carefully to remove the hidden quills close to the skin
2. Empty them, wash them and clean them well
3. Stuff them with a mixture of their cooked and chopped giblets, beaten eggs, toasted and grated bread, ground almonds, Málaga raisins, pepper, salt, cumin, and a piece of butter

title: Pichones a la Delicieux (Squabs Delicieux)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Fine herbs
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Toasted bread soups in boiling consommé
- Giblets (cooked and chopped)
- Beaten eggs
- Toasted and grated bread
- Ground almonds
- Málaga raisins
- Salt
- Cumin
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pluck the squabs carefully, removing all hidden feathers close to the skin.
2. Clean and empty them thoroughly, then rinse well.
3. Prepare the filling by combining the cooked and chopped giblets, beaten eggs, toasted grated bread, ground almonds, Málaga raisins, pepper, salt, cumin, and a piece of butter.
4. Cook this mixture in a frying pan over fire for ten minutes, stirring constantly to incorporate all ingredients.
5. Fill the squabs with this mixture.
6. Season the filled squabs with salt and pepper, then coat with butter.
7. Wrap each squab in buttered paper.
8. Place in a well-heated oven until cooked.
9. Prepare the sauce by grinding parsley and mixing it with juice from a tomato roasted over hot coals and strained through a sieve.
10. Season the sauce with salt, pepper, a few drops of vinegar, and a good amount of pure olive oil.
11. Serve the squabs with the parsley sauce.

title: Pichones a la Negrita (Squabs Negrita Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Two onion heads
- Celery branches
- Salt
- Flour
- Butter
- Bird stock
- Bird blood
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the squabs with two onion heads, celery branches, and salt.
2. Prepare a sauce by making a roux with flour and butter, then add the bird stock and the blood (collected in a cup with a tablespoon of vinegar to prevent coagulation).
3. Season the sauce with salt and pepper.
4. Place the cooked squabs in a serving dish with the celery and peeled onion heads.
5. Pour the sauce over the squabs.

title: Pichones a la Nevada (Squabs Nevada Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Butter
- Olives
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Almonds
- Raisins
- Salt
- Pepper
- Puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the squabs.
2. Fill them with olives, halved hard-boiled eggs, almonds, and raisins, seasoning with salt and pepper.
3. Fry the filled squabs in butter.
4. Once fried, wrap each squab in puff pastry dough.
5. Place in the oven for rapid cooking of the pastry.
6. Serve hot, bringing from oven to table.
7. This dish can also be served as an excellent cold dish.

title: Pierna de Carnero a la Napoleon (Leg of Mutton Napoleon)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- pickled onions
- pepper
- fresh bacon
- butter
- onions, halved
- half bottle of red wine
- half bottle of vinegar
- water
- rosemary branch

## Directions

1. Make deep incisions in the fleshy parts and insert a filling of pickled onions, pepper, and fresh bacon
2. Cook in an enamel porcelain pot with butter, halved onions, pepper, half a bottle of red wine, half a bottle of vinegar, water to cover the meat, and a bunch of rosemary
3. Bring to a gentle boil until the liquid has evaporated and the meat is golden

title: Piña a la Bogotana (Bogotá-Style Pineapple)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pineapple
- cinnamon
- clove
- ground sponge cake
- four eggs
- butter
- one glass of Burgundy wine
- cinnamon
- clove
- toasted and ground sugar

## Directions

1. Cook a pineapple; once cooked, peel it, grind it, and mix with powdered cinnamon and clove and ground sponge cake in the quantity necessary to form a paste with the pineapple. Wrap this with four well-beaten eggs, and pour into a buttered mold and cook in the oven. Serve over a sauce made with one glass of Burgundy wine, mixed and boiled with a seasoning of powdered cinnamon and clove and toasted and ground sugar.

title: Pollo Apanado (Breaded Chicken)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One fat four-month-old chicken
- Salt
- Water
- Chicken gizzard
- Chicken liver
- Chicken heart
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Kill a fat four-month-old chicken by cutting off its head
2. Collect the blood, which with the gizzard, liver and heart will be cooked with two boils in lightly salted water
3. Chop everything finely and season it to serve as filling for the chicken
4. Pluck and wash the chicken, dry it by pressing it in a napkin
5. Coat it entirely with a feather in a mixture of oil and vinegar beaten with pepper, salt and ground parsley
6. Roll it in bread crumbs and place it on the grill or roaster with a good fire, but not too lively
7. When the breading begins to crust, turned once or twice, coat it with the oil and vinegar mixture, and put it back on the fire, increasing the heat
8. Do this up to three times, in which you will let it take on a golden toast, a sign of being ready to serve
9. Coat it very lightly with the mixture again when sending it to the table

title: Pollos al Coñac (Chicken with Cognac)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Chicken broth
- Flour
- Butter
- Cognac
- Gruyere cheese

## Directions

1. Cook the chickens
2. With their broth, make a sauce as follows: take one tablespoon of flour, which is dissolved in butter and add the necessary broth to regulate the thickness
3. Arrange the chickens in pieces on a platter
4. Pour a good glass of cognac into the sauce, pour it over the pieces
5. Grate Gruyere cheese over it, so as to cover them, and put them in the oven, from where they are removed when the cheese has browned

title: Ponche a la Joanes (Joanes' Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of sugar
- lemon peel
- hot water
- juice of two lemons
- one bottle of good cognac
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Rub pieces of sugar on the peel of ripe lemons
2. Use these to sweeten the hot water in the punch bowl
3. Add the juice of two lemons
4. Add one bottle of good cognac
5. Serve hot in cups, placing two thin slices of lemon in each cup

title: Ponche a la Laurita (Laurita's Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four spoonfuls of powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- one glass of water
- one glass of wine or other liquor

## Directions

1. Put four spoonfuls of powdered sugar with cinnamon into a bottle
2. Add one glass of water and one glass of wine or other liquor
3. Chill thoroughly
4. Cork the bottle and shake strongly until the liquid has turned to foam
5. Serve immediately

title: Ponche a la Parnell (Parnell Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bottle of rum
- 2 lemon peels
- sugar
- 1 liter of sweetened tea

## Directions

1. Infuse for one hour in 1/2 bottle of rum the peels of 2 lemons after rubbing them on a piece of sugar
2. Prepare at the same time an infusion of 1 liter of tea sweetened to taste, with the piece of sugar rubbed with lemon included
3. Remove the peels that are infusing and mix the rum to the tea infusion which should be boiling

title: Ponche Andino (Andean Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- six to twelve glasses of orange or lemon sorbet
- water sweetened with burned sugar
- cinnamon
- powdered nutmeg
- rum, cognac, or grape brandy

## Directions

1. Pour six to twelve glasses of orange or lemon sorbet into a punch bowl
2. Add water sweetened with burned sugar, cinnamon, and powdered nutmeg to taste
3. Add rum, cognac, or grape brandy in desired quantity
4. Stir to incorporate everything
5. Cover the punch bowl with a folded napkin and let infuse for one hour
6. When serving, place a small piece of snow in each cup

title: Ponche Celestial (Celestial Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 lemon peels
- 1/2 bottle of cognac
- juice of 30 lemons
- 2 bottles of cognac
- 4 pounds of powdered sugar
- 4 bottles of distilled water
- 1 bottle of boiling milk

## Directions

1. Cut the peels of 9 lemons so thin that they are transparent, put them in 1/2 bottle of cognac, and let soak 24 hours
2. Mix the juice of 30 lemons with 2 bottles of cognac, 4 pounds of powdered sugar, 4 bottles of distilled water, and 1 bottle of boiling milk, add to the entire previous infusion, stirring much to prevent coagulation
3. Then, let cool and pass through a flannel strainer
4. Put in a vessel and let ferment for 10 days

title: Ponche de Huevo (Egg Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve egg yolks
- juice of two lemons
- one glass of Jamaican rum or cognac
- one liter of hot brewed tea, sweetened with sugar
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat together twelve egg yolks and the juice of two lemons
2. Add, little by little and without ceasing to beat, one glass of Jamaican rum or cognac if rum is unavailable
3. Mix into one liter of hot brewed tea already sweetened with sugar
4. After incorporating everything well, beat with a molinillo
5. Add six egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
6. The action of the molinillo in the comforting liquid will create delicious foam in each cup

title: Ponche Frio (Cold Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of powdered sugar
- 1 lemon, cut in slices
- 1 cup of rum, Jamaica brandy or cognac
- 1 bottle of good white wine

## Directions

1. Mix in a punch bowl and, well covered, let infuse for half an hour before drinking

title: Ponche Olimpico (Olympic Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one coconut from Panama or Brazil, or four dozen coconuts from Paraguay
- half spoonfuls of milk
- two dozen blanched and washed almonds
- one liter of hot cream
- sugar
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- one egg white
- half bottle of Jamaican rum

## Directions

1. Grate first, then grind in a mortar one coconut from Panama or Brazil, or in its absence, four dozen coconuts from Paraguay
2. While grinding in the mortar, add milk little by little, half spoonfuls at a time
3. Add two dozen blanched and washed almonds every four additions, grinding them with the coconut
4. Once ground, strain through a strong cloth sieve
5. Press well to extract the thickest juice
6. Mix the juice with one liter of hot cream sweetened with sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg
7. Immediately and quickly pour a beaten egg white (whipped to stiff peaks) onto this mixture, beating with a molinillo (traditional beater)
8. Add half a bottle of Jamaican rum after the egg white
9. To keep this punch hot, since the coconut prevents reheating over fire, place the bowl over a vessel of boiling water
10. Beat each cup when serving and top with foam

title: Ponche Olímpico (Olympic Punch)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Panama or Brazil coconut, or 4 dozen Paraguay coconuts
- milk
- 2 dozen peeled and washed almonds
- 1 liter of boiling cream of milk
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate first and then grind in a mortar a Panama or Brazil coconut, or failing this, 4 dozen Paraguay coconuts
2. While grinding in the mortar, add little by little half spoonfuls of milk, at the same time as, every four spoonfuls, 2 dozen peeled and washed almonds, which are ground with the coconut
3. Once ground, strain in a sieve of strong cloth
4. Press well to make them release their thickest juice, which is mixed with 1 liter of boiling cream of milk, and seasoned with sugar and cinnamon

title: Preparación para la Carne (Meat Preparation)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- meat: beef or poultry
- salt
- thin cloth napkin
- twine

## Directions

1. Wash and prepare the piece of meat: beef or poultry, enclose it in a thin cloth napkin, which will be gathered at its ends, tying it with twine, leaving the meat inside loose and ample but well sealed.
2. Dig a hole in clean, humid earth, for example in a garden or vegetable patch, and bury the meat thus sealed in the napkin for three hours.
3. Then remove it carefully so that no earth enters it, dusting the napkin with a feather duster before untying the twine.
4. Place the meat on a plate, season it with necessary salt, and extend it on the grill, or give it the required destination.
5. By this operation, the meat obtains an exquisite flavor and becomes so tender that a brief boil is sufficient for its perfect cooking, as well as ten minutes of lively fire on the roaster.

title: Ranas a la Gaditana (Frogs Prepared in the Cadiz Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Frog thighs
- Butter
- Flour
- Mushrooms, cut into small pieces
- Fine herbs, chopped
- Salt and pepper
- Half a glass of white wine
- Good broth
- Two or three egg yolks, beaten

## Directions

1. Briefly blanch frog thighs in boiling water for one minute.
2. Cool them in ice water for one minute.
3. Dry them and fry them slowly in butter.
4. Dust them with flour using a spoon, add a handful of mushrooms cut into small pieces, chopped fine herbs, salt, and pepper.
5. Add half a glass of white wine and some spoonfuls of good broth.
6. When the sauce has reduced, the cooking is complete.
7. Remove the pan from the heat and arrange the frog thighs with the mushrooms in a serving dish.
8. Remove the fine herbs from the sauce with a skimmer.
9. Add two or three beaten egg yolks to the sauce and pour it very hot at the moment of serving.

title: Rebozado de Cabrito (Breaded Kid Goat)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- un cuarto de cabrito (a quarter of kid goat)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- aceite (oil)
- pimienta (pepper)
- sal (salt)
- orégano (oregano)
- ajo molido (ground garlic)
- harina (flour)
- seis huevos (six eggs)
- agua (water)
- papel fuerte (strong paper)
- mantequilla (butter)
- pan (bread)
- jugo de tomate (tomato juice)
- crema espesa de leche cruda (thick cream of raw milk)
- cucharada de vinagre (spoonful of vinegar)
- tres cucharadas de aceite de oliva (three spoonfuls of olive oil)

## Directions

1. Prepare a quarter of kid goat that has recently finished nursing
2. Place it for two hours in ordinary marinade: vinegar, oil, pepper, salt, oregano, and a little ground garlic
3. Prepare separately a well-consistent paste with flour, six eggs, and necessary water to make it manageable; once the paste is well kneaded, let it rest one hour wrapped in damp linen
4. Extend the paste with a rolling pin, forming a cake about one centimeter thick
5. Remove the kid goat leg from the marinade, dry it very well, and wrap it in the paste, moistening the edges of the mass to join them together
6. Cover the wrapped meat with strong paper untreated in butter and place on the spit with moderate heat
7. After three hours of cooking, remove the paper so the paste browns
8. Serve accompanied separately with a sauce made of bread crumbs dissolved in tomato juice, thick cream of raw milk, one spoonful of vinegar, three spoonfuls of olive oil, salt and pepper; beat all until completely incorporated

title: Rebozado de Sesos (Breaded Brains)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- sesos (brains)
- sal (salt)
- pimienta (pepper)
- pan frío (cold bread)
- huevo batido (beaten egg)
- especias (spices)
- mantequilla (butter)

## Directions

1. Cook the brains and remove fibers and membranes
2. Season with salt and pepper, and place between slices of cold bread
3. Wrap in beaten egg conditioned with spices and fry in hot butter over moderate heat
4. When they brown, serve very hot

title: Rebozados a la Helenita (Helenita's Breaded Dish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- mantequilla (butter)
- pan rallado (grated bread)
- queso rallado (grated cheese)
- una, dos o tres cucharadas de crema (one, two or three spoonfuls of cream)
- leche (milk)
- las yemas de dos huevos (yolks of two eggs)
- pimienta (pepper)
- sal (salt)
- huevos (eggs)

## Directions

1. Place a piece of butter in a plate; mix grated bread and cheese with it, one, two or three spoonfuls of cream, or lacking that, good milk, the beaten yolks of two eggs, pepper, and very little salt because of that which the cheese contains
2. Mix all this very well, beating it, and place over fire in an enameled pot over a layer of butter spread on the bottom of the pot
3. When cooking begins to manifest on the surface, break over it as many eggs as desired, taking care that they fall in their form, one beside the other, covering the frying paste
4. Sprinkle pepper, a little salt, and grated cheese over them; cover the pot with its lid, spreading embers and coals over it
5. When the cheese and eggs have taken on a light golden color and the whites have turned white, serve in the same pot, on a serving platter, hiding the edges with sprigs of parsley and mint

title: Reina Mora (Moorish Queen)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty egg whites, beaten until fluffy
- two pounds of ground almonds
- three biscuits
- four pounds of powdered sugar
- butter
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix and knead, making a paste of thirty egg whites beaten until fluffy, two pounds of ground almonds, three biscuits, and four pounds of powdered sugar
2. When the paste is united and smooth, place it in a mold with a beautiful form, buttering the bottom and sides with a layer of butter
3. Sprinkle with cloves and cinnamon, and place in the oven
4. Serve hot or cold

title: Relleno a la Manolita (Manolita-Style Stuffed Lamb Legs)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb legs, cleaned and peeled
- Ground meat mixture with raisins, crushed almonds, ground roasted peanuts, salt, and pepper
- Pork fat
- Butter
- Ground veal
- Two tablespoons of finely chopped fine herbs
- Flour
- Warm water
- Lemon
- Butter
- Lean ham, chopped into small pieces
- Veal, chopped into small pieces
- Two chicken legs, chopped into small pieces
- Salt, pepper, and spices
- Bay leaf
- Two carrots
- Two onions
- Six hard-boiled egg yolks, well ground
- Milk

## Directions

1. Clean the lamb legs and carefully remove the upper bone with a pointed knife without tearing the meat.
2. Fill the hollow with a picadillo seasoned with raisins, crushed almonds, roasted and ground peanuts, salt, and pepper.
3. Stuff the legs and sew them well closed to prevent the filling from escaping.
4. In a pan, melt a piece of pork fat and a piece of butter.
5. Cook ground veal and two tablespoons of finely chopped fine herbs in this mixture.
6. After two boils, add one tablespoon of flour, one cup of hot water, and a lemon cut into thin slices.
7. Stir well and after two more boils, pour this preparation over the legs arranged at the bottom of a pan.
8. Let them cook for four hours.
9. Remove from heat and drain the legs, remove the stitching thread, place them in a serving dish.
10. To prepare the sauce: bring to a slow boil in a pan 250 grams of butter, 25 grams of lean ham, 500 grams of veal, and two chicken legs, all chopped into fine pieces.
11. Season with salt, pepper, spices, a bay leaf, two carrots, and two onions.
12. When the meats have taken on color in the butter, add half a dozen well-ground hard-boiled egg yolks, stirring vigorously to blend the mixture.
13. Gradually pour in one liter of milk, stirring constantly, and let it cook at a slow fire for one hour, stirring always.
14. Pass through a fine sieve and pour hot over the stuffed legs at the moment of serving.

title: Repollo a la Romana (Cabbage Roman Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- sausage meat
- poultry offal cooked
- bread crumb
- pepper
- cumin
- two egg yolks
- olive oil
- vinegar
- butter
- chicken livers
- bacon slices
- sauce cooked
- parsley
- shallot
- mushrooms cooked
- truffles cooked
- good broth
- Champagne wine
- pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. Choose a well-formed cabbage and, passing a string around it to prevent it from opening, blanch it in salted water.
2. When it has received this first, light cooking so that the leaves do not lose their firmness, remove it from the water, receive it in a cloth, with which gently press it to dry it; place it standing on a plate; remove the string that closes it, and open it slightly in the shape of a rose.
3. Prepare a filling as follows: grind in a mortar sausage meat, cooked poultry offal, and bread crumb, seasoned with pepper, a pinch of cumin and salt if that of the sausage is not sufficient.
4. Add two half-beaten egg yolks to incorporate and bind the filling. Add half a spoonful of oil, half a spoonful of vinegar and a piece of butter the size of a hazelnut. Mix very well.
5. With this filling, coat the interior and exterior of each cabbage leaf. Then, with light and careful hand, fold the leaves back over the heart, also filled with minced chicken livers. The cabbage thus recovers its form.
6. Tie a string around it again to preserve it; and in a tall pot so as not to crush the cabbage in its cooking, putting on the bottom a layer of bacon slices sprinkled with cooked sauce, place the cabbage always standing; cover it with the lid, with hot coals on top, and moderate heat below, cook slowly.
7. Serve with a sauce made as follows: Put in a pot four spoonfuls of good broth with a little chopped parsley; a shallot; cooked mushrooms and truffles cut into thin slices, and a spoonful of butter, seasoning with salt and pepper. Stir, letting it boil for half an hour during which the broth will have reduced. Then add a spoonful of oil. Stir for a moment, and add half a glass of Champagne wine. Stir for half a minute; remove the pot from the fire and arrange in the serving dish, in which the cabbage is immediately served standing, wedged with pickled gherkins.

title: Revoltijo (Mixed Salad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- ham
- cabbage
- onion heads
- potatoes
- carrots
- green beans
- green pickled peppers
- radishes
- olives
- hard-boiled eggs
- bread fried in butter
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- lettuce hearts and leaves

## Directions

1. Cook all ingredients well, then chop finely the beef, ham, cabbage, onion heads, potatoes, carrots, green beans (stripped of their film), green pickled peppers, radishes, olives, hard-boiled eggs, and bread fried in butter.
2. Mix and blend everything well, pouring over it by spoonfuls the oil and vinegar beaten with pepper and salt.
3. Once prepared, arrange the salad as a beautiful mosaic, placing as garnish four tender lettuce hearts at the center of the salad bowl, and leaves of four others around the edges, all bathed in the mixture of beaten oil and vinegar.

title: Ricura (Almond Custard)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of almonds, finely ground
- 1 pound of sugar
- 2 cups of water
- 6 eggs, beaten

## Directions

1. Make a syrup in high point with 1 pound of sugar and 2 cups of water
2. Pour the ground almonds into the boiling syrup, stir
3. Add the beaten eggs to the mixture
4. Fill a sponge cake with this paste, carefully hollowing out the center
5. Place in a regularly heated oven

title: Ricura (Deliciousness)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of very well ground almonds
- one pound of sugar
- two glasses of water
- six eggs

## Directions

1. Make a high-point syrup with one pound of sugar and two glasses of water
2. In this boiling syrup, add one pound of very well ground almonds, stir and add the beaten eggs
3. Fill a sponge cake with this paste by hollowing out the center carefully
4. Place in the oven at regular heat

title: Riñoncitos a la Radical (Veal Kidneys Radical)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- flour
- white wine
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the veal kidney lengthwise into two halves.
2. Cut crosswise into very thin slices.
3. Place them with butter in a pan and fry, seasoning with salt and pepper.
4. Once fried, add a cup of broth with flour dissolved in it.
5. Add half a glass of white wine and a bit of chopped parsley.
6. Let cook for a quarter of an hour.
7. Serve with lemon juice.

title: Salchicha (Sausage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat, finely chopped
- bacon, finely chopped
- vinegar
- four heads of garlic, ground
- pepper
- cumin, ground
- onion
- oregano powder
- salt
- two bay leaves
- sausage casings

## Directions

1. Chop the pork meat very finely and chop bacon as well
2. Place in a wooden tub or terra-cotta pot to marinate for twenty-four hours with the same mixture as for the adobo: vinegar, ground garlic, pepper, ground cumin, chopped onion, oregano powder, salt, and bay leaves
3. Stuff the mixture into sausage casings, tied at intervals of a third
4. Hang to dry in a shady, well-ventilated place, taking care not to hang in the kitchen where smoke would impart an unpleasant odor

title: Salchichón a la Rosalia (Rosalia-Style Sausage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat, finely chopped
- pork bacon, finely chopped
- vinegar
- garlic, ground
- salt
- pepper
- cumin
- peanuts, ground
- almonds, ground
- walnuts, ground
- bread, pulverized
- oregano, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- thick sausage casing
- string

## Directions

1. On a board, chop pork meat and bacon very finely with a machete and place in a terra-cotta or wooden vessel to macerate in vinegar for twenty-four hours
2. Drain the vinegar until the meat is dry, then mix well with ground garlic, salt, pepper, cumin, peanuts, almonds, walnuts, pulverized bread, and chopped oregano and parsley
3. Mix everything very well with a spoon and stuff into thick sausage casings tied at intervals, forming a horseshoe shape
4. Boil in water for two hours, then remove from fire, dry with a cloth, and let dry for a few hours in a cool place before storing

title: Sandwichs a la Pitina (Pitina-Style Sandwiches)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin (sirloin), 4 centimeters thick
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Garlic, ground
- Bread crumbs soaked in cream
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Beef marrow
- Parsley
- Eggs, beaten
- Butter
- Lettuce hearts
- Olive oil and vinegar for dressing

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of veal loin 4 centimeters thick and as long as needed according to the number of sandwiches required.
2. Let it marinate for two hours in a marinade of oil and vinegar seasoned with pepper, salt, oregano, cumin, and ground garlic.
3. Cut into slices 2 centimeters thick and lightly pound each slice with the flat of a knife, laying them on a cloth to receive the filling.
4. Make the filling by grinding together in a mortar bread crumbs soaked in cream, hard-boiled egg yolks, beef marrow, and parsley, seasoned with salt and pepper.
5. Spread a spoonful of this filling on one slice of meat and cover with another, brushing the meat edges with beaten egg to prevent the filling from coming out.
6. Wrap each sandwich in paper greased with butter and roast on a grill, or in the oven if permitted.
7. Serve arranged in a crown around the edge of the platter, with a small cluster of lettuce hearts dressed in oil and vinegar, seasoned with salt and pepper, in the center.

title: Sangría Congelada (Frozen Sangria)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Six mature lemon peels, cut very thin
- Water, quantity corresponding to three glasses
- Pieces of good cinnamon
- Sugar, sufficient amount to sweeten well
- Two egg whites
- A pinch of nutmeg powder
- One bottle of good red wine (Burgundy or Bordeaux)

## Directions

1. Cut the lemon peels very thin and place them in infusion for two hours in the quantity of water corresponding to three glasses, together with pieces of good cinnamon and sufficient sugar to sweeten well.
2. Strain through a sieve.
3. Beat two egg whites and mix them with a pinch of nutmeg powder.
4. Pour a bottle of good red wine (Burgundy or Bordeaux) over all of this.
5. Freeze using the customary procedure.

title: Seviche (Ceviche)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One corbina (white fish)
- Juice of 30 sour oranges
- Onion heads, sliced very thinly
- Ground ají (chili pepper)
- Pepper
- Salt
- Oregano
- Ground cumin

## Directions

1. Select a beautiful corbina, a fish with white and appetizing flesh; descale it, open it, remove the spines and wash it repeatedly with cold water.
2. Cut it into regular square chunks like large dice.
3. Previously prepare a sauce made with the juice of 30 sour oranges, onion heads cut into very thin slices, plenty of ground ají, pepper, salt, oregano, and ground cumin.
4. Place the corbina chunks on a platter, pour the sauce over them, toss each piece of corbina in the sauce, and let them pickle in the strong juice of the sour orange—which serves as cooking—for eight hours, and serve immediately.

title: Siberiana (Siberian Dessert)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds, blanched and soaked in cold water
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Grind very well in a mortar one pound of blanched almonds that have been soaked in cold water
2. Once ground, mix with milk and stir well
3. Strain through a clear cloth, pressing strongly so the almond releases all its juice
4. Grind the resulting pomace again, and mixing it with more milk, strain again through linen in another portion of milk
5. Combine all the strained liquid, sweeten appropriately, place on the heat and stir until it has boiled for five minutes
6. Once boiled, pour into a large porcelain cup, and before serving, place it between ice for one hour

title: Sollito Mojarra (Grilled Small Mojarra)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- sollitos (small whole fish)
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- fine herbs
- bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Clean and season with salt and pepper
2. Wrap in paper spread with butter and roast on grills
3. Once cooked, remove the paper and fill with butter mixed with finely chopped fine herbs, bitter orange juice, salt and pepper
4. Serve hot as they come from the grills

title: Sopa Aderezada (Dressed Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- bacon
- ham
- poultry giblets
- hard-boiled eggs
- grated cheese
- parsley
- whole pepper
- butter
- broth
- hard-boiled eggs for garnish
- artichoke hearts
- fried small birds

## Directions

1. Cut bread into thin slices and toast them carefully on a grill
2. Chop bacon, ham, poultry giblets, and hard-boiled eggs with grated cheese, parsley, and whole pepper
3. Season with butter and cook over fire for ten minutes
4. In a casserole, layer toasted bread and chopped bread, alternating until the casserole is half full
5. Mix the sauce with the broth and pour it over the bread
6. Place over fire until it comes to a boil
7. Serve with slices of hard-boiled eggs, artichoke hearts, and fried small birds as garnish

title: Sopa de Abril (April Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- lettuce
- meat
- chorizo
- tender fava beans
- onions
- oregano
- parsley
- salt
- tomato juice
- pork fat or butter
- broth
- bread, thinly sliced and toasted on grill

## Directions

1. Chop carrots and lettuce in equal quantities
2. Add equally chopped meat and chorizo, and tender fava beans
3. Cook seasoned with onions, oregano, parsley, salt to taste, and tomato juice in pork fat or preferably butter
4. When it comes to a boil, pour good broth over it
5. Cook for half an hour
6. Place a layer of thin bread slices toasted on the grill in the bottom of the soup tureen
7. Fill with the soup
8. Let it rest a moment and serve

title: Sopa de Albondiguillas (Meatball Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of milk
- two ounces of butter
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of pepper
- wheat flour
- butter for frying
- hot broth
- pepper
- one beaten egg

## Directions

1. Break four eggs into a bowl and beat them with one cup of milk, two ounces of butter, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of pepper
2. Mix wheat flour into this mixture until a consistent dough forms
3. Shape the dough into small meatballs the size of an olive
4. Roll the meatballs in flour and fry them in butter
5. Place the meatballs in a soup tureen and pour hot broth over them, to which pepper and a beaten egg have been added

title: Sopa de Albondiguillas (Meatball Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup milk
- two ounces butter
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of pepper
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Break four eggs in a bowl and beat them with one cup of milk, two ounces of butter, a pinch of salt and a pinch of pepper
2. Mix wheat flour into this beaten mixture until a consistent dough forms
3. Form small meatballs the size of an olive from the dough
4. Coat them in flour
5. Fry in butter
6. Place the meatballs in the soup tureen and pour very hot broth over them

title: Sopa de Arroz (Rice Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, one ounce per person
- warm water
- good meat broth
- onions, thinly sliced
- tomato
- whole pepper
- garlic clove, ground
- bell pepper
- oregano
- parsley, chopped
- pork fat or butter
- salt
- fresh cheese slices
- hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- fried sausage, sliced

## Directions

1. Calculate one ounce of rice per person
2. Wash the rice with warm water, rubbing it well
3. Cook it slowly in good meat broth
4. Make a sauce with thinly sliced circular onion slices, tomato, whole pepper, one ground garlic clove, bell pepper, chopped oregano and parsley, and the corresponding pork fat or butter and salt
5. When the onion has browned after coming to a boil, add broth from the pot and put it on the fire
6. When it begins to boil, add the rice already cooked beforehand
7. When serving, place fresh cheese slices, hard-boiled egg slices, and fried sausage slices in the tureen

title: Sopa de Camarones (Shrimp Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimp shells
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- broth
- bread crumbs
- bread crumbs
- egg yolk
- nutmeg
- butter
- shrimp tails

## Directions

1. Place shrimp shells, except those from the head, in the oven to toast well
2. Crush them until they become powder
3. In a pan, melt one tablespoon of butter over fire
4. When melted, mix in the shrimp shell powder along with one tablespoon of flour and stir with a few tablespoons of broth
5. Remove from fire, add broth and bread crumbs
6. Return to fire and let boil
7. Strain through a sieve and if too thick, add more broth
8. When serving, add meatballs made by beating one tablespoon of butter and three eggs until foamy, adding salt and nutmeg, and gradually mixing in bread crumbs to form a thick paste; cook these in separate broth
9. Also add peeled shrimp tails to the soup

title: Sopa de Gallina (Hen Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- hen
- salt
- onion slices
- two tablespoons of flour
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the hen with salt to taste and onion slices
2. When the broth is ready, remove the fat from the surface
3. In this fat, dissolve two tablespoons of flour
4. Beat well and add the necessary broth for the soup
5. Add the pieces of hen and after a light boil, add a glass of good white wine before serving

title: Sopa de Gallina con Alverjas (Hen Soup with Peas)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- hen
- salt
- celery
- garlic clove
- parsley
- lettuce leaves
- raw peas
- two ounces of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- bread fried in butter
- hen meat

## Directions

1. Cook the hen with very little salt, celery, and one garlic clove until tender
2. Grind together parsley, lettuce leaves, and raw peas
3. Put two ounces of butter and two tablespoons of flour in a saucepan and melt them over fire, stirring
4. Add the vegetable mixture and let rest for ten minutes at low heat
5. Pour the hen broth over this mixture and let it boil
6. The soup should be somewhat thick
7. Strain into a soup tureen over pieces of bread fried in butter and pieces of hen meat

title: Sopa de Tortuga (Turtle Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds turtle meat
- three ounces butter
- four garlic cloves, ground
- two ounces roasted peanuts, ground
- onion, sliced
- tomato juice
- parsley, chopped
- salt
- four cups good broth
- one glass wine
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash two pounds of turtle meat well
2. Place it in an enameled pot
3. Fry with three ounces of butter, adding four ground garlic cloves, two ounces of roasted and ground peanuts, sliced onion, tomato juice, chopped parsley and corresponding salt
4. When everything is well fried, add four cups of good broth and a glass of wine
5. Season and let it boil until well cooked
6. Sprinkle with pepper in the soup tureen when serving

title: Sopa de Té para Desayuno (Tea Soup for Breakfast)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- bread slices
- sugar
- cinnamon
- clove
- strong tea infusion
- good milk

## Directions

1. Spread fresh butter on bread slices that will cover the bottom of the soup tureen
2. Sprinkle sugar, cinnamon, and a very small amount of clove on top
3. Pour a very strong tea infusion and double the quantity of good milk over it

title: Sopa Salteña (Salta-Style Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of cold bread
- fresh cheese or grated Gruyere cheese
- fried chorizo, sliced
- seasoned broth
- onions, finely chopped
- garlic clove, ground
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut very thin slices of cold bread and toast them on the grill
2. Layer the toasted bread in an enameled porcelain skillet
3. Over each bread layer, place a layer of fresh cheese slices or grated Gruyere and fried chorizo slices
4. Over each of these layers, pour two kitchen spoonfuls of broth seasoned with a sauce of finely chopped onions, one ground garlic clove, and pepper
5. When the skillet is filled with these layers, cover with a layer of grated cheese and fried chorizo slices
6. Cover with a lid and cook over low heat
7. Just before serving, break as many eggs on top as there are people at table
8. Once the eggs are cooked, serve in the same skillet placed on a tray, with parsley branches adorning the edges

title: Sopa Teóloga (Theologian's Soup)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey meat
- chicken meat
- squabs
- beef
- lamb
- head and feet of meat
- handful of chickpeas
- salt
- bread, cut into dice shape
- pork fat or butter
- carrots
- green peas
- cabbage
- onions
- salt and hot water
- bird giblets (livers, hearts, gizzards, and ovaries)
- hard-boiled eggs, sliced

## Directions

1. Make broth with turkey meat, chicken, squabs, beef, lamb, head and feet, and a handful of chickpeas, seasoned with salt to taste and cooked slowly in a covered pot
2. Place diced bread pieces, fried in pork fat or butter, in the soup tureen beforehand
3. Cook carrots, green peas, and cabbage separately in a separate broth
4. Soak onions in salt and hot water
5. Chop all the bird giblets (livers, hearts, gizzards, and ovaries) into small pieces
6. Add slices of hard-boiled eggs
7. Pour the very hot broth over all this
8. Cover the tureen again and let it rest for ten minutes before serving

title: Sábalo a la Mimosa (Mimosa-Style Shad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- sábalo (a fish)
- brine
- butter
- fine herbs
- pepper
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- white wine
- lemon

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and empty the sábalo, then place it in brine in an earthenware pot for one hour, turning it occasionally so that it salts evenly
2. Select a fire-resistant serving dish and place a layer of butter and finely chopped fine herbs on it
3. Place the sábalo on this layer, dusting it on both sides with pepper and nutmeg
4. Cover with a layer of breadcrumbs, moisten with a bottle of white wine
5. Add another layer of breadcrumbs and pour melted butter over the sábalo, which should not be fully covered by the wine
6. Place in the oven, opening it occasionally to check the cooking and baste it with its own broth
7. Serve in the same dish in which it was roasted, accompanied by lemon halves for those who wish to season it with juice

title: Sábalo a la Natural (Natural-Style Shad)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- sábalo (a fish)
- salt
- water
- butter
- fine herbs
- pepper

## Directions

1. After scaling, opening, and cleaning the sábalo, soak it for one hour in well-salted water
2. Mix butter with a handful of finely chopped fine herbs and proper seasoning of salt and pepper
3. Fill the interior of the sábalo with this mixture
4. Make two lengthwise cuts along the backbone on the back and place the herb-butter mixture inside these cuts as well
5. Wrap the fish in paper spread with butter and cook it on a grill over moderate heat
6. When well cooked, remove from paper and serve, sprinkled with lemon juice, accompanied by a sharp sauce of either mustard with tomato juice or oil and vinegar with green chili peppers

title: Tamal Limeño (Peruvian Corn Tamale)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of white corn
- quicklime
- salt to taste
- lard from pork
- four eggs
- cooked pork meat
- hard-boiled eggs
- ground ají
- bacon
- almonds
- ground peanuts
- corn husks
- water

## Directions

1. Cook four pounds of white corn in plenty of water with handfuls of quicklime to remove its skin.
2. After two boils, drain the water and place the corn in a trough or open vessel.
3. Rub and wash the corn alternately and many times until it is clean of skins, white, and without trace of lime.
4. Grind it until reduced to a paste and season with necessary salt, plenty of pork lard, and four eggs, kneading the mass until it has acquired consistency and softness.
5. Divide the mass into pieces of the size the tamales are to have, and fill them with a picadillo of cooked pork meat seasoned with hard-boiled eggs, ground ají, bacon, almonds and ground peanuts.
6. Wrap them in corn husks, which are prepared by soaking them to give them flexibility and washing them well.
7. Once wrapped, place them with little water in a pot, arranging them in squares like building a wall.
8. Cover the mouth of the pot very well, first with burlap folded in four, then with the lid, so that steam helps with cooking.
9. Cook for two hours over slow fire, adding once or twice a little hot water if the water in the pot has diminished.
10. After two hours of slow cooking, the tamales are ready to serve.

title: Tecti (Fermented Beverage)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Water, one arroba
- Rice, one pound
- Roasted peanuts, one pound
- Walnuts, one pound
- Sweet almonds, four ounces
- Toasted corn, one handful
- Fennel, one small bunch
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Clove
- Orange leaves

## Directions

1. Cook in one arroba of water: one pound of rice, one pound of roasted peanuts, one pound of walnuts, four ounces of sweet almonds, all well cleaned and ground, adding one handful of toasted corn and one small bunch of fennel
2. When it has cooked sufficiently, pass through a strainer and place to ferment, seasoned with sugar, cinnamon, clove, and orange leaves
3. Three days of fermentation are sufficient to produce a delicious beverage

title: Tocino Acaramelado (Caramelized Ham Rind)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- ham rind with fat
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Once the ham is cool, sprinkle it very well with powdered sugar mixed with a bit of cinnamon
2. Pass a hot iron over it on top of paper

title: Tocino Guisado (Stewed Ham Rind)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- ham rind, cut into small pieces
- butter
- white onion
- tomatoes
- parsley, finely chopped
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the ham rind into small pieces
2. Give them a boil in a sauce made of butter, white onion, tomatoes and finely chopped parsley
3. Break eggs over the top
4. Season with salt, pepper and one tablespoon of vinegar
5. Stir until the egg is cooked and serve

title: Tomatada (Tomato and Egg Dish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe tomatoes
- Onion heads
- Butter
- Pepper
- Salt
- Beef broth, good quality, 1 cup
- Raw cream, 1 cup
- Vinegar, 2 tablespoons
- Good olive oil, 1 tablespoon
- Bread, toasted on grill, 3-4 slices
- Eggs, 1 per person
- Parsley sprigs for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook together ripe tomatoes and onion heads.
2. Remove seeds from the tomatoes and cut into regular pieces; cut the onions into slices.
3. Place them in an enameled pot to season over low heat for a while with a piece of butter, pepper and salt, stirring for ten minutes.
4. Pour over them one cup of good broth and one cup of raw cream.
5. Stir again; add two tablespoons of vinegar and one of good oil.
6. Submerge three or four slices of bread toasted on a grill in this sauce.
7. Break eggs over this as if to fry them, regularly one per person.
8. Cover the pot and leave it on the fire for one minute.
9. Bring to the table in the same vessel, which should be placed on a platter, festooned with parsley sprigs to cover the edges.

title: Tomates a la Abadesa (Tomatoes Abbess Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- truffles
- ground cooked meat
- hard boiled eggs
- melted butter
- fine bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes in half widthwise; remove the seeds; carefully scoop out the pulp; pass it through a sieve and incorporate it into a filling prepared with truffles, minced cooked meat, and chopped hard boiled eggs.
2. Fill the tomatoes with this preparation.
3. Put melted butter on the bottom of a heat-resistant baking dish; place the tomatoes in this dish with a thick layer of very fine bread crumbs.
4. Complete the cooking on low heat with hot coals in the dish cover and coals underneath.

title: Torta Argentina (Argentine Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of well-ground sugar
- one pound of wheat flour
- one pound of fresh butter
- twelve eggs
- lemon essence
- dulce de leche (milk preserve)
- half a pound of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare tin molds the size of a large plate with a wire around the edge in the form of a rim or border.
2. Beat the twelve eggs, reserving three egg whites.
3. Beat well the twelve egg yolks and nine egg whites with the butter, and once beaten, add the flour, cupped by cupped, after passing it through a sieve; then add the sugar.
4. All together, beat until it has become a smooth and soft mass.
5. Add a few drops of lemon essence.
6. Lightly butter the tins or molds, and cover with a very thin layer of dough, so thin that we shall call them hosts, and place them in a strong oven, which cooks them momentarily.
7. Once cooked, turn the molds over onto a table so that the hosts come out intact.
8. There should be twelve molds, extracting the hosts, and set aside the twelfth for the base.
9. On the host of this mold, spread a layer of dulce de leche, and on top of this a host.
10. On it another layer of dulce and a host.
11. Thus, alternately, until the twelve leaves that complete the cake, which will end with a host.
12. Beat in meringue point three egg whites with half a pound of powdered sugar; coat the cake with this beaten mixture and place it in a warm oven to dry the coating.

title: Torta de Almendras (Almond Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound pulverized sugar
- One pound peeled almonds, finely ground
- Twelve eggs
- Half ounce cinnamon
- Butter for the mold
- Six egg whites
- Half pound pulverized sugar for the glaze
- Cinnamon for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat the twelve egg yolks and two egg whites.
2. Continue beating until thickened, then mix in the sugar and beat again.
3. Add the ground almonds and stir, beating with two spoons to incorporate both substances while sprinkling with half an ounce of cinnamon.
4. Butter a mold or heat-resistant dish and place the batter in it.
5. Bake in a hot oven until cooked.
6. Remove from the oven and coat with a meringue made from six egg whites and half pound of pulverized sugar, sprinkled with cinnamon.
7. Return to a warm oven to dry the glaze.

title: Torta de Natas (Cream Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of a liter of good milk
- half a pound of sugar
- six egg yolks
- cinnamon powder
- three dozen slices of sponge cake
- cream
- egg threads
- white candies

## Directions

1. Prepare a quarter of a liter of good milk, which after a boil, let it cool.
2. Add half a pound of sugar and six egg yolks half-beaten, seasoning with cinnamon powder.
3. Cut three dozen slices of sponge cake, soak them in that milk, and arrange a layer of them at the bottom of a dish.
4. On this layer of sponge cake, spread another of cream, on which pour a strong sprinkling of sugar and cinnamon.
5. Then another layer of sponge cake, and another of cream, alternating until the dish is filled with one of sponge cake, and place it in the oven for a quarter of an hour, with moderate heat.
6. Serve with reliefs of egg threads and white candies.

title: Torta Real (Royal Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough
- Dulce de leche or manjar blanco (milk candy)
- Peach preserves
- Sour cherry or strawberry preserves
- Peeled almonds, ground and seasoned with sugar and cinnamon
- Lemon preserves
- Syrup at high point
- Candied fruits for decoration
- Angel hair (spun sugar) for decoration

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry dough and cut three molds the size of the dish in which the confection will be made.
2. Spread one layer in the bottom of the dish and bake in the oven until cooked; remove and place another layer, baking it equally; remove when ready and bake the third layer.
3. On the bottom puff pastry layer, spread a layer of dulce de leche or manjar blanco.
4. Over this, spread a layer of peach preserves.
5. Place the second puff pastry layer on top.
6. Arrange a layer of sour cherry or strawberry preserves and a layer of almond paste (peeled almonds, ground and seasoned with sugar and cinnamon).
7. Add another layer of lemon preserves.
8. Top with the third puff pastry layer.
9. Coat the top with syrup at high point and bake in the oven for a quarter hour at moderate heat.
10. Decorate with candied fruit reliefs and angel hair or spun sugar.

title: Torta Real (Royal Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- dulce de leche (milk preserve)
- peach preserve
- cherry or strawberry preserve

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry dough, and cut three molds from it the size of the dish in which the preparation is to be made.
2. Spread one at the bottom.
3. Place it in the oven, and once cooked remove it; place another and put it equally in the oven, from where, once ready, remove it to cook the third.
4. On the puff pastry at the bottom of the dish, spread a layer of dulce de leche or milk preserve; on top of this, another of peach preserve, and after this, the second puff pastry, on which arrange a layer of cherry or strawberry preserve, and a layer

title: Torta Vizcaína (Basque Cake)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Thirty egg yolks, well beaten
- One soup plate of cream
- Four ounces ground sugar
- Half a medium-sized sponge cake (bizcochuelo), ground
- Butter for the mold
- Egg white batter for dipping
- Butter for frying
- Syrup
- Almonds, raisins, and candied fruits for decoration

## Directions

1. Mix the thirty well-beaten egg yolks with one soup plate of cream and four ounces of ground sugar, adding half a ground medium-sized sponge cake.
2. Beat the mixture together until it thickens.
3. Place in a buttered mold and bake in the oven until cooked.
4. Remove from the oven and cut into slices.
5. Dip the slices in a batter made from egg whites, then fry them in butter.
6. Roll the fried slices in syrup and arrange them on a dish in a wheel shape, with the slices as spokes and decorations of almonds, raisins, and candied fruits.

title: Tortilla a la Hija del Aire (Daughter of the Air Omelet)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cream or milk
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- onion
- lard
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Break the desired number of eggs - keeping in mind that two fill a plate. Having broken the eggs, separate in different vessels the yolks and whites, and beat them: the yolks just enough for them to break down; the whites to stiff peaks: that is, until they become light and consistent foam.
2. Mix them in a cup of raw cream, or good milk, salt to taste, pepper, and a clove of garlic and a slice or two of onion, ground in the mortar.
3. Put in a pan a piece of lard on high heat. When the lard is boiling, lightly mixing the yolks, the whites in foam and the milk, stir and fry.
4. Since the cooking of this omelet is very quick, due to its lightness, a moment later it is necessary to divide it in a cross with the tip of a knife, and with two forks turn each of the four parts so that they finish frying.
5. When serving it, give it its shape, making the action of the knife disappear with a relief of sliced potatoes.

title: Tortilla de Natas (Cream Omelet)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- milk
- salt
- parsley
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Break the number of eggs according to the size you wish to give the omelet. Beat separately the yolks and the whites: the latter to stiff peaks, the former as for sponge cake.
2. Meanwhile, put a good piece of butter in a pan on the fire. When it begins to boil, quickly mix yolks and whites; stir so that they incorporate at the same time as you season with salt and chopped parsley, and fry.
3. Since its lightness causes rapid cooking, turn it from bottom to top, lifting it with the blades of two knives, and arrange on this cooked surface a layer of cream, which a moment later will be covered by folding the omelet in the shape of a turnover.
4. Serve with cooked potatoes, placed around as reliefs alternating with sprigs of parsley.

title: Tortilla de Riñones a la Clarita (Kidney Omelet Clara Style)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen eggs
- lamb kidneys
- butter
- fine herbs
- roast juice
- Jerez wine
- salt
- pepper
- hot sauce or mustard sauce or tomato sauce with green chilies

## Directions

1. Beat twelve eggs to foam, and put half in a pan with butter, to cook as an omelet on moderate heat, turning it so that it cooks evenly.
2. In the meantime, prepare the filling by cutting lamb kidneys into small pieces and cooking them in butter, fine herbs finely chopped, four spoonfuls of roast juice, and half a glass of Jerez wine: all with seasoning of pepper and salt.
3. Next, spread the filling over the omelet, and pour the rest of the beaten egg over it, adding a piece of butter, and covering the pan with its lid, on which will be placed hot ash and embers.
4. Once the omelet is cooked, serve it with hot sauce, either mustard or tomato with green chilies.

title: Tortilla Rellena (Stuffed Omelet)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- white onion
- garlic
- cumin
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat together the whites and yolks of the eggs needed for the omelet; season this mixture with pepper and salt, and have ready a finely chopped filling seasoned with chopped white onion, pepper and a little garlic and cumin.
2. Butter the bottom and sides of a pan, put it on moderate heat, and pour in half of the beaten egg.
3. When cooking is evident on the surface, arrange the filling on top; spread it, and pour all the rest of the beaten egg over it. Increase the heat, and after also buttering it, put the lid on and flip it, so that the lid is over the fire and the omelet finishes cooking and browning.
4. Serve in the pan, placed on a platter, with the edges hidden with sprigs of parsley.

title: Tortilla à la Bretonne (Breton Omelet)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen eggs
- butter
- mushrooms
- flour
- roast juice
- white wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. With a dozen beaten eggs, make an omelet fried in butter, being careful to lift it with two forks so that it cooks evenly.
2. When half cooked, remove part of the center, being careful not to perforate it. Fill this hollow with mushrooms previously prepared as follows:
3. Make a sauce of butter with a spoonful of flour dissolved in a little roast juice; half a glass of white wine, and the juice of a lemon. Cook the necessary mushrooms, cut into small pieces, in this sauce.
4. Once cooked, remove the mushrooms with a skimmer and fill the omelet, folding it over itself to cover the filling. After stirring, finish cooking it. Serve it, sprinkled with the sauce in which the mushrooms have cooked.

title: Tortuga a la Turca (Turkish-Style Turtle)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle meat
- vinegar, one bottle
- oil, two or three tablespoons
- salt
- pepper
- oregano
- garlic, half a head, ground
- onions, two heads, sliced
- green chiles, four, deseded and cut into small pieces
- English mustard or yellow chile sauce
- roasted tomato juice, strained
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the turtle meat into thin slices and place in a marinade for six hours
2. Prepare the marinade in a wooden, ceramic, or enameled earthenware vessel: combine one bottle of vinegar, two or three tablespoons of oil, salt, pepper, oregano, half a head of ground garlic, two heads of sliced onions, and four green chiles, deseded and cut into small pieces
3. Arrange the turtle meat slices in this marinade, submerge them well, and let them cure for the stated time
4. After curing, drain and fry in an enameled pan, due to the vinegar from the marinade
5. After five minutes of boiling over a lively fire, the frying is done and should be served with piquant sauce, either English mustard or yellow chile, but always in roasted tomato juice passed through a sieve and with sour orange juice

title: Valdiviano (Chilean Wake-Time Dish)
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- Charqui (dried beef)
- Onions, chopped
- Bread, sliced
- Potatoes, fried
- Water, boiling
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Roast and grind the charqui (dried beef) in a mortar until shredded.
2. Arrange it in a platter by layers, alternating with chopped onions and slices of bread and fried potatoes.
3. Pour boiling water over this, then drain it.
4. Pour oil and vinegar over the top, seasoning everything with salt and pepper.
5. Serve with halved hard-boiled eggs around the edges.

title: Vol-au-Vent à la Lionese
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- fat chicken
- butter
- onion, chopped
- salt
- white wine, one glass
- flour, three tablespoons, fried in butter
- fried sausage pieces
- small meatballs of meat and egg, spiced and fried
- asparagus tips
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Cut up a fat chicken and fry in a pan with butter
2. Once fried, add a little chopped onion, a little salt, and one glass of white wine and continue cooking for five minutes
3. Arrange the pieces on a serving dish and strain the broth in which it cooked, adding three tablespoons of flour fried in butter to thicken it
4. On the dish, alternating with the chicken pieces, place fried sausage pieces and small meatballs of meat and egg seasoned with pepper and fried, as well as asparagus tips, and pour the sauce over
5. Cover this filling with a layer of puff pastry and place in the oven, which should be at high heat for rapid cooking

title: Walnut Paste Cake
author: Juana Manuela Gorriti
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound peeled and ground walnuts
- breadcrumbs from a criollo bread, grated
- 12 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar
- slices of sponge cake
- 1 cup sweetened milk
- cinnamon
- 4 tablespoons ground walnuts
- 3 egg whites
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar for meringue
- gold threads or spun eggs in syrup
- candies for decoration

## Directions

1. Mix the ground walnuts with the grated bread crumbs, egg yolks, and powdered sugar
2. Beat the mixture well
3. Prepare a layer of sponge cake slices soaked in nogada (sweetened milk sprinkled with cinnamon and mixed with ground walnuts passed through a sieve)
4. Arrange the walnut paste on this base
5. Bake in a strong oven for about fifteen minutes
6. Remove from the oven and coat with a frosting made from three egg whites and half a pound of powdered sugar beaten to meringue point
7. Return to a warm oven so the frosting dries
8. Decorate with gold threads or spun eggs in syrup, alternating with candies
